races.race_name: Andy Thornton Hospitality Furniture Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Great Dream
runners.spotlight: Won an AW maiden at Newcastle (7f) last September and ended his 3yo campaign for the Crisford stable with a good of 13 in a Yarmouth handicap (7f, good to soft) in October; having been gelded he was sent off favourite to make a winning start for William Haggas over 1m at Salisbury three weeks ago, but failed to pick up having had to switch to get a run; quickly reverts to 7f and remains with potential for his new yard; stall 18 not ideal, though.
runners.comment: Possibly stretched by 1m when beaten favourite on recent yard debut; retains potential

runners.name: Jannas Journey
runners.spotlight: Had a wonderful 2025, winning seven times at 6f-1m on a range of surfaces; back on last winning mark and first sign of encouragement this year when running on for fourth of nine over 7f at Carlisle (good to firm) 13 days ago; player if she can build on that.
runners.comment: Seven wins in 2025; first promise this year when fourth latest; claims if building on it

runners.name: Inspired
runners.spotlight: 1m AW winner last December, caught the eye back from a gelding operation when fourth (Quest For Fun third) of 22 over C&D (good) last month, finishing well after repeatedly meeting trouble; couldn't fully confirm that promise but still ran respectably when fourth again (Orne third) at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) last Thursday; one to consider.
runners.comment: Eyecatcher when fourth over C&D last month; fair fourth at Wetherby since; considered
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood

runners.name: Tokyo Joe
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a breakthrough win in novice company at Lingfield (6f, AW) in December; returned with a respectable sixth of 18 in 6f Ascot handicap (good to firm) five weeks ago and he's entitled to build on that now stepping back up in trip.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with 6f AW novice win; returned with fair sixth of 18 in 6f Ascot handicap

runners.name: Count Palatine
runners.spotlight: Successful over 7f at Carlisle (good to firm) and Ayr (soft) in the second half of last season; left his first two efforts of the new campaign behind when third of ten back at Carlisle (1m, good) 13 days ago; claims if he can build on that from a handy inside stall.
runners.comment: Two 7f wins last year and back on form when third over 1m at Carlisle latest; nicely drawn

runners.name: Lir Speciale
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2025 campaign with a heavy-ground 7f double at Doncaster; started this season with good thirds at Doncaster (22-runner Spring Mile; good to soft) and Chester (7.6f, good) but needs to bounce back from a lesser effort at Newbury last time; any easing of the ground would be in his favour; connections have tasted recent success in this.
runners.comment: Started year with two good thirds but poor effort latest; seems best on good or softer

runners.name: Oriental Prince
runners.spotlight: In good order on AW over the winter but made no more than a respectable return to turf after a break when fifth of six at Hamilton 5f, good) 16 days ago; has done the majority of his racing over sprint trips but was successful over 7f last year.
runners.comment: Made only a respectable return from break 16 days ago but may come on for the run

runners.name: Native Honey
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come over 7f at Ayr, surging back to form when scoring there on good to firm last month; a lesser run over 6f has followed but a revival isn't ruled out back at 7f from a good draw.
runners.comment: Second 7f Ayr win last month and might pay to forgive a lesser 6f run since

runners.name: Eligible
runners.spotlight: Veteran who won over 7f at Southwell in January but has been operating below his best since; drawn widest and likely best to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Veteran who has been below best since AW win in January; drawn widest

runners.name: Criminal Shore
runners.spotlight: No great strike-rate but a switch to hold-up tactics worked a treat when landing a second career win at Newcastle (1m, AW) ten days ago, doing well to get up having not got in the clear until inside the final furlong; a shorter trip and different track mean similar tactics may not be as easy to pull off this time.
runners.comment: Hold up tactics worked a treat at Newcastle (AW) latest but different trip and surface now

runners.name: Feel The Need
runners.spotlight: Good C&D record includes a win in this race (good to firm) off 1lb higher last year; good second at Southwell (7f) in January but always behind over C&D four months later; may well have needed that outing but needed to see more to be confident.
runners.comment: Won this off 1lb higher last year but always rear on return from break 20 days ago

runners.name: Esherann
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won two 1m races (good) in Ireland last year, but hasn't seen his race out in three outings over that trip for Declan Carroll this spring; now drops to 7f for the first time since his debut.
runners.comment: Has faded in three 1m outings since coming from Ireland and now drops back to 7f

runners.name: The Green Man
runners.spotlight: Has raced almost exclusively on Newcastle's AW since joining Phil Kirby last autumn, winning three times (6f/7f) and placed on six other occasions, including last four starts; three 6f turf wins earlier in career (including here on good to firm) provide hope that he can continue the good work now back on the grass.
runners.comment: Had a good spell on AW since joining this yard and course winner earlier in career
runners.owner: The Dalby Family And P Kirby

runners.name: Leadman
runners.spotlight: Has an underwhelming strike-rate, given his ability, and his two C&D runs last month (both good ground) rather sum him up, shaping very well when second of 22 at the Dante meeting but never involved when favourite eight days later; could pop up but it's likely things will need to drop this hold-up performer's way.
runners.comment: Second over C&D at Dante meeting but never involved when favourite back here since

runners.name: Quest For Fun
runners.spotlight: Plenty of C&D form in big fields, notably wins on good to soft in July of 2023 and 2025; behind Leadman when fifth at the Dante meeting last month but well in front of David O'Meara's charge and looked a bit unlucky (met trouble) when third of 22 back here three weeks ago; solid.
runners.comment: Plenty of big-field C&D form, shaping well (met trouble) when third of 22 latest; solid

runners.name: Orne
runners.spotlight: Smart earlier in career; yet to win for this yard but gave it a good shot from the front when third over C&D (good) at the Dante meeting and bounced back from a lesser effort over 6f here when third (Inspired fourth) of 14 at Wetherby (good to firm; made running again) last week; each-way claims again.
runners.comment: 0-11 for yard but good efforts from front when third over C&D and at Wetherby this spring

runners.name: Lord Bertie
runners.spotlight: Encouraging return when sixth of 19 at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) in March, but didn't back that up when last back there (6f again) three weeks ago; only 1lb above his winning mark from last autumn but he'll need things to fall right from stall 20.
runners.comment: On a workable mark but he'll needs things to fall his way from stall 20

runners.name: Mister Sox
runners.spotlight: His six successes include four last season; his second at Thirsk (6f, good to firm) in May shows he retains all his ability and two lesser efforts since have resulted in him dropping to 1lb below his last winning mark; has done most of his winning over 6f but scored over 7f in 2024.
runners.comment: Not at best last twice but he has dipped below last winning mark, so dangerous to discount

runners.name: Wobwobwob
runners.spotlight: Losing run stretches back over two years; best effort this year when fifth of eight over 7f at Goodwood (soft) on Sunday, but he'll need more if he's to exploit his reduced mark.
runners.comment: Sunday's Goodwood fifth doesn't suggest he's in good enough form to snap losing run

runners.name: Sword
runners.spotlight: Third in last year's International at Ascot (7f, good) off this mark, but he's not straightforward and last month's reappearance for a new stable with a tongue-tie added (retained) was disappointing (reported to have lost a shoe); drawn wide and can only really watch.
runners.comment: Talented but quirky and recent reappearance/yard debut was disappointing

runners.name: Our Havana
runners.spotlight: Recorded useful wins on AW and turf last spring; not in the same form later in 2025 but has dipped back to a good mark as a result and he's gone well fresh before; no surprise were he to stage a revival back from wind surgery.
runners.comment: Out of form at end of 2025 but back on good mark and gone well fresh before; had wind op

races.race_name: Constant Security Keeping York Racegoers Safe Rous Selling Stakes

runners.name: Everatease
runners.spotlight: Improving race by race and the step up to 6f and drop to selling company saw him run out an easy winner at Chester 13 days ago (good ground); had Undercover Affair and Furturra, both winners since, in behind on that occasion and meets them on the same terms; major player.
runners.comment: Improved for 6f when winning a Chester seller 13 days ago (form franked); strong claims

runners.name: Tamam Star
runners.spotlight: Shaped well on his Musselburgh debut but fluffed his lines when odds-on for a Thirsk maiden one week later; got back on track when third of seven at Carlisle two weeks ago (5f, good) and stepping up to 6f should be in his favour; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Beaten at odds-on on second run but first/third starts both bring him into the reckoning

runners.name: Undercover Affair
runners.spotlight: He wasn't well drawn when second to Everatease at Chester (6f, good) 13 days ago; won a 5f seller at Goodwood on Sunday; versatile groundwise and he shouldn't be far away once more.
runners.comment: Second to Everatease at Chester before a ready 5f win on Sunday; shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Turnstile
runners.spotlight: Ran as well as he ever has when third to stablemate Furturra at Musselburgh on Saturday (5f, good); step back up to 6f not sure to play to his strengths and a career-best performance is required.
runners.comment: Good 3rd to stablemate Furturra at Musselburgh on Saturday (5f, good); more needed to win

runners.name: Furturra
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when landing a 5f seller at Musselburgh on Saturday; put in his place by Everatease at Chester beforehand and he will need a career best to follow up.
runners.comment: Won a 5f seller on Saturday but held by Everatease on earlier 6f run at Chester

runners.name: Lady Dublin
runners.spotlight: Two fourth-place finishes in 5f events at Beverley this spring (both good to soft), failing to build on her debut promise on the second occasion (2-1 favourite); represents a trainer/jockey combination seeking a hat-trick in the race; can do better at 6f.
runners.comment: Fourth in two 5f runs at Beverley; trainer/jockey combination have won last two runnings

runners.name: Baila Morena
runners.spotlight: She hasn't achieved a huge amount in form terms in her two runs but last month's Redcar fourth (6f, good) was a step in the right direction and the run can be upgraded as she didn't have optimal track position; one of four runners for Ollie Pears.
runners.comment: Not well positioned when fourth of 15 at Redcar last month (6f, good); can do better
runners.owner: T&t Racing Ltd And Keith West

runners.name: Court Legend
runners.spotlight: £20,000 yearling; seventh foal; brother to 6f AW winner Kapparis Kid (including 2yo; RPR 73), closely related to 5f/6f AW winner Beneficiary (70), half-brother to 7.5f-9.4f winner Locked N'Loaded (86); dam placed 1m1f in France; one of two newcomers for the stable; betting instructive.
runners.comment: £20,000 yearling with three winning siblings; yard run two newcomers; betting to guide

runners.name: Mick's The Boy
runners.spotlight: 3,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to 5f/7f 2yo winner Gray's Inn (RPR 88); unraced dam from a good middle-distance family; one of two newcomers for the yard.
runners.comment: 3,000gns half-brother to 2yo winner Gray's Inn (RPR 88); yard run two newcomers

runners.name: Hey Up Ronnie
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns foal, 4,000euros yearling; fifth foal; Ubettabelieveit half-brother to winners Zebelle (5f 2yo; RPR 80) and Decanter (Czech 1m4f); dam unraced half-sister to useful French winners Disclose (1m), World Ruler (7f/1m) and Grand Vista (5.5f/6f 2yo); trainer has a fine record in the race but his four winners all had the benefit of experience.
runners.comment: 4,000euros yearling; half-brother to two winners; one of four runners for Ollie Pears

runners.name: State Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Only 9-2 for her debut but finished well beaten; slightly better at Carlisle a fortnight ago but others bring stronger claims.
runners.comment: Needs to leave her two 5f efforts well behind to come out on top

runners.name: Jimmy You Mushroom
runners.spotlight: Poor form in two 5f runs at Beverley last month; new trip/headgear not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Poor form in two 5f runs at Beverley last month; new trip/headgear not enough to tempt

runners.name: Explainingislosing
runners.spotlight: Displayed some promise in his first two stars and he was drawn wide when down the field in Everatease's Chester race 13 days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Has hinted at ability but he needs to raise his game a level to take this

runners.name: Enemy Action
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and slowly away on his Beverley debut (5f, good to soft) 39 days ago, keeping on late to take sixth (just behind Explainingislosing); open to improvement and his trainer's record in this race (won four of the last nine runnings including the last three) piques interest.
runners.comment: Needs to improve on Beverley debut 6th but that seems likely; stable target race and run 4

runners.name: Martial Order
runners.spotlight: Beat just one rival home in his two runs for Karl Burke last month; sold for 4,000gns last week; others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Beat one rival in two runs for Karl Burke last month; sold 4,000gns last week; opposable

races.race_name: British EBF Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Bintaryana
runners.spotlight: Returned from a six-month break to win a 6f fillies' maiden at Nottingham in decisive fashion; beaten at short odds twice since while leaving the impression she was still learning her craft; return to 6f can help; capable of better.
runners.comment: Beaten twice at short odds since her reappearance win; unexposed and commands respect

runners.name: Shallow
runners.spotlight: Good second over 7f at Yarmouth in April (cheekpieces first time); over 4l behind Beaujolais Nouveau at Ripon last month and a stiffer test at the trip might be preferable.
runners.comment: Good 7f run in April but no match for Beaujolais Nouveau at Ripon last time

runners.name: Cool Molly
runners.spotlight: In fine form on AW this year and she continued the good work back on turf when third of 12 at Chester's May meeting (7.6f, good); well beaten at Goodwood three weeks ago; also engaged 3.12 Chester Friday.
runners.comment: Enjoying a good year but she needs to rebound from a modest Goodwood run

runners.name: Siouxpersonic
runners.spotlight: 5f AW win for Ed Bethell last September; better form when a close second at Ripon on her seasonal/stable debut at Ripon (6f, good); not so good at Hamilton 16 days ago when tried in a hood; now gets cheekpieces and needs to improve for them.
runners.comment: Good 2nd on seasonal/stable debut; not quite so good latest; new headgear today

runners.name: Lady Roxby
runners.spotlight: Solid effort in a 6f handicap at Kempton on her reappearance but she has come up short in two runs since (stiff task latest); could prove vulnerable once more.
runners.comment: Not at her best on her last two starts and vulnerable to less-exposed rivals

runners.name: Splish Splash
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; easy win at Haydock last October and backed it up with a fine third in a Newmarket Listed race two weeks later (both 6f on good ground); little to shout about in two runs this year but she should find this company more manageable.
runners.comment: Progressive 2yo but she hasn't fired in 2026, albeit in better races; still early days

runners.name: Dandana
runners.spotlight: Won 6f novice events at Kempton and Ripon on her first two starts and ran a creditable race when fourth in the Group 2 Lowther Stakes over C&D last August; flopped on soft ground when last seen 265 days ago but that is easy enough to excuse; still has time to do better.
runners.comment: Ran well in a C&D Group 2 last summer; absent nine months but far from exposed

runners.name: Mercury Day
runners.spotlight: Won twice at around 1m in June 2024; ran well dropped to 6f at Newmarket last June but absent for 283 days afterwards and finished well held at Kempton on her return to action in April; enough to prove for now.
runners.comment: Her two wins came at around 1m in June 2024; low-key reappearance; others are stronger

runners.name: Rhyme Dust
runners.spotlight: Ran well when third in a Listed event at Ayr last September (5.5f, soft; final run for Mark Usher); unplaced in two Listed races for new stable in 2026; faces a more suitable challenge today and her apprentice takes off a valuable 7lb.
runners.comment: The return to handicap company can help and her rider's claim is a bonus; each-way shout

runners.name: Beaujolais Nouveau
runners.spotlight: Returned to her best when winning a handicap with something to spare in first-time cheekpieces at Ripon (6f, good) four weeks ago; a 7lb rise asks a stiffer question of her today though.
runners.comment: Ready 6f win at Ripon last month in cheekpieces for the first time; 7lb rise demands more

runners.name: Cotai Belle
runners.spotlight: Chased home Beaujolais Nouveau at Ripon four weeks ago (6f, good) and she is 6lb better off for 2.25l; may find a couple stronger today.
runners.comment: 2nd to Beaujolais Nouveau and 6lb better off today; might find one or two stronger

runners.name: Eternal Sunshine
runners.spotlight: Got on a roll in the second half of 2025, completing a quick hat-trick when landing the Portland Handicap at Doncaster (5.6f, good) in September; yet to get going this year and she is due to be 3lb lower from the weekend; hood now added.
runners.comment: Yet to recapture 2025 highs so far this year; now hooded; will be 3lb lower from Saturday

races.race_name: Juddmonte EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice (Bands A-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive)

runners.name: Big Negotiator
runners.spotlight: Very solid market leader when promising third on her introduction at Navan (5f, yielding) in May and her supporters were out again when second at Listowel (6.5f, good) 12 days ago; can probably cope with this drop back down in trip and she sets the standard.
runners.comment: Promising third (5f) in May followed by second (6.5f) 12 days ago; sets the standard

runners.name: Call Me Tomorrow
runners.spotlight: Went down narrowly on her Newmarket debut in April and, after a blip over that C&D next time, she was back on track when second to a well-supported newcomer at Salisbury (also 5f, good) three weeks ago; considered.
runners.comment: Went down narrowly on April debut; back on track (also 5f, good) latest; considered

runners.name: Cailin Aine
runners.spotlight: All starts at 5f; promise in the first three of them and she put it all together when justifying favouritism at Musselburgh (good) three weeks ago; sales-price allowances mean she still holds a fighting chance despite 6lb penalty.
runners.comment: Promise first three starts; won latest; penalty but allowances give her a fighting chance

runners.name: Sable Island
runners.spotlight: 33-1 but it was a promising start when a head behind Quiet Soul at Southwell (5f, AW) in April; didn't build on that when again third at Bath (5.5f, firm) 17 days ago; still early days.
runners.comment: Didn't build on promising start when again third 17 days ago; still early days

runners.name: May Bee In Profit
runners.spotlight: Similar profile to stablemate Cailin Aine in that she showed promise before getting off the mark on her fourth start, in this case at Carlisle (5f, good) a fortnight ago; also has her penalty offset by allowances and warrants respect.
runners.comment: Similar profile to stablemate Cailin Aine; also has penalty offset by allowance; respected

runners.name: Quiet Soul
runners.spotlight: 33-1 but it was a good first go when second at Southwell (AW, Sable Island a head behind) in April; didn't build on that when last of three on turf debut at Ayr (again 5f, good to firm) next time but the runner-up subsequently won the Hilary Needler and the winner is a leading fancy for Royal Ascot next week; purchase price means she gives weight to all, however.
runners.comment: Promising debut; well held behind two good ones on turf next time; concedes weight to all

runners.name: Divine Whisper
runners.spotlight: 30,000gns yearling, £30,000 breeze-up 2yo; Mehmas filly; half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Spirit Of Leros (RPR 90); dam once-raced sister to winners Ektihaam (1m4f Listed) and Music Box (7f Group 3); yard going well with its juveniles and top-class rider Zac Lloyd is already off the mark on his British sojourn; market a big help on this debut.
runners.comment: £30,000 breeze-up 2yo; by Mehmas; good yard going well with its 2yos; market a big help

runners.name: Time Glory
runners.spotlight: 13-2 and there was encouragement despite evident inexperience when fourth of nine at Redcar (6f, good to firm) 17 days ago; drop down to 5f not an obvious positive on pedigree but she'll know more this time and can likely do better.
runners.comment: Encouragement despite inexperience when fourth (6f) 17 days ago; can likely do better

runners.name: Sovereign Grace
runners.spotlight: By Invincible Spirit; 43,000euros yearling; fifth foal; closely related to French 7f 2yo/7.5f winner Red Smile (RPR 98); dam French 1m4f winner, half-sister to 2yo winners Byron Bay (6f) and Hyper Hyper (6.5f), out of 1m 2yo winning half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Giofra; hails from a powerful yard whose newcomers always command respect; betting helpful.
runners.comment: By Invincible Spirit; 43,000euros yearling; strong yard and needs close market inspection

runners.name: Givendale
runners.spotlight: Far from disgraced despite flashing her tail when fifth on her debut at Beverley in May but well down the field at Redcar next time; work to do despite getting all the allowances.
runners.comment: Down the field at Redcar last time; work to do despite getting all the allowances

races.race_name: Sky Bet The World's Gone Football Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Urban Glimpse
runners.spotlight: Went close in C&D handicap (good) at last year's Dante meeting in May; had been gelded when one of three of these who didn't see which way the winner went at Goodwood in May but better effort, albeit well held, when second at Beverley (1m2f, good) a fortnight ago; not fully exposed and gets first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Went close in C&D handicap lat May; not fully exposed and gets first-time cheekpieces

runners.name: Antipodes
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who returned a very useful RPR when taking a Windsor maiden (1m) around a year ago; subsequently gelded and he returned with a winning handicap debut over that C&D (also good to firm) 18 days ago; has some stamina in the family and this step up in trip should be no problem for this unexposed sort.
runners.comment: Winning h'cap debut (1m) 18 days ago; step up in trip likely no issue for unexposed sort

runners.name: Dark Moon Rising
runners.spotlight: 18 starts here have yielded five places and a win over 1m4f back in 2023, although he wasn't at his best when beating one home in this race last year; in fair form over this trip on AW in the spring but his return to turf, when beaten 8l over 1m4f here one month ago, will need improving on.
runners.comment: Return to turf, when beaten 8l over 1m4f here one month ago, will need improving on

runners.name: Altareq
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in three starts for the Gosdens and it was a cracking stable/handicap debut when third of 15 over C&D (good) at this year's Dante meeting in May; solid show when third at Hamilton last Thursday, despite a drop in distance couting against him, and he's very interesting returned to this trip.
runners.comment: Cracking stable/handicap debut when third over C&D in May; interesting back at this trip

runners.name: Castle Stuart
runners.spotlight: Dual winner for Karl Burke who posted respectable sixths over 1m2f (including here) on first two starts of the year for this yard; raced freely when disappointing over a stiff 1m3f a fortnight ago and the return to this trip looks a positive; added hood may help too.
runners.comment: Disappointed on his last start but return to this trip and added hood could be positives

runners.name: Rainbow Nebula
runners.spotlight: Secured the latest of his three wins when taking Redcar handicap (1m, good) in May; stepped up to this trip for the first time when staying-on fifth in the Zetland Gold Cup back there 18 days ago and this fairly progressive sort should have more to offer.
runners.comment: First go at this trip when staying-on fifth at Redcar last time; should have more to offer

runners.name: Love Beach
runners.spotlight: Haydock maiden winner on last year's return and he again made a successful reappearance when impressively taking ten-runner Nottingham handicap (1m2f, good) in April; bit below that form when beaten 8l at Goodwood (City Of Delight second, Urban Glimpse behind) off this mark six weeks ago, however.
runners.comment: Another successful reappearance when winning (1m2f, good) in April; bit below that latest

runners.name: City Of Delight
runners.spotlight: Rattled off a four-timer (good to firm/good/soft) in 2024 and landed his first two starts of 2025, latterly the City & Suburban (also 1m2f) at Epsom in April; just one good run from four starts in Meydan this winter but his British return, when second at Goodwood with two of these behind six weeks ago, could be a run to build on.
runners.comment: 6-21 and British return, when second at Goodwood 41 days ago, might be a run to build on
runners.owner: All For One Racing III & I Bandres

runners.name: Rathgar
runners.spotlight: Very close to his best when beating 13 others to land Newbury handicap in April and he made all at Epsom (also 1m2f, good) ten days later; may have been fortunate to beat a subsequently impressive winner on the latter occasion, however, and this career-high mark demands a fair bit more.
runners.comment: On a hat-trick but may have been fortunate last time; career-high mark demands more

runners.name: Spioradalta
runners.spotlight: Better than ever when he won five times last year and he's carried on that good work this term, scoring at Ripon (1m2f, good to soft) in April and again over that C&D (good) a fortnight ago; that's arguably his favourite track but he's got form here too and can't be dismissed in current mood.
runners.comment: Better than ever this year with two Ripon wins; has form here too and he's in calculations

runners.name: Cloth Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Placed in all three qualifying runs at two and after a disappointing handicap debut in September, when coming back from an absence, he made the breakthrough at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) 16 days later; subsequently gelded and he brings an unexposed profile to this reappearance.
runners.comment: Made breakthrough (1m2f, AW) when last seen; gelded and unexposed ahead of reappearance

runners.name: Per Contra
runners.spotlight: He went very close in 15-runner handicap over C&D (good to firm) at the Ebor meeting in August and secured the latest of his four wins when bagging 1m2f AW handicap five weeks later; solid effort when keeping-on third of 12 at Carlisle (1m3f) a fortnight ago and he should be on the premises.
runners.comment: Went close in handicap over C&D in August; solid third latest; should be on the premises

runners.name: Sportingsilvermine
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who won his first two starts for this yard; took four runs to get going this term but he was strong in the betting when cashing in on a reduced mark at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; handicapper has wasted no time putting him back to this career-high mark, though.
runners.comment: Justified market strength off reduced mark a fortnight ago; handicapper didn't like that

runners.name: Astro King
runners.spotlight: Top handicap form when nosed out in the John Smith's Cup over C&D and again when scoring at the Ebor meeting and picking up the Cambridgeshire in 2023; 0-7 since and he beat one home on his return from a hefty absence/wind surgery at Newmarket in May; should build on that comeback but may need another run or two before becoming of interest.
runners.comment: Top handicap form in 2023; beat one home on return from hefty absence/wind surgery latest

runners.name: Fantasy Believer
runners.spotlight: Ten-time winner who was close to a lifetime best when bagging Doncaster handicap (1m2f, soft) at the Leger meeting in September; still marooned on this career-high mark despite two subsequent defeats but his form figures after a 50-day break or more read 32112913 and he won't mind if the rains come.
runners.comment: Form figures after a 50-day break or more read 32112913; won't mind if the rains come

runners.name: Wahdan
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent individual but he was placed in a French Group 2 when trained there in 2024; beat a total of two horses in his last four starts for Joseph O'Brien and left that yard for 8,000gns in October; subsequently gelded and makes his stable debut with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Disappoining for previous yard and sold for 8,000gns in October; has been gelded

races.race_name: Dawson Williamson Architects Handicap

runners.name: Vinciamo
runners.spotlight: He was having his first run back from wind surgery when second to Forest Phoenix at Catterick (5f, good to firm; made the running); 2lb better off with his old rival now handicapping, and this track should suit, but it still might not be enough to turn the tables.
runners.comment: Picked off late by Forest Phoenix at Catterick; 2lb better off; this track should suit

runners.name: Houndhill
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the eighth attempt - his first run in a visor - when fending off 11 rivals at Newcastle last month (5f, AW; Class 6; front pair clear); a 6lb rise against stronger opposition could find him out.
runners.comment: Visored first time when winning a Class 6 at Newcastle last month; up 6lb and two classes
runners.owner: J Raybould N Skinner D Jenkins

runners.name: Forest Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three maiden/novice runs in April (7f, good and 6f, AW); dropped to 5f at Catterick 13 days ago and came from off the pace to win with far more authority than the neck margin might suggest; should get a strong pace to aim at and there could be more in the tank; interesting.
runners.comment: Cosy win dropped to 5f at Catterick 13 days ago; moves into handicaps with potential

runners.name: I'm Dan Dare
runners.spotlight: Showed promise for Adrian Nicholls and he had plenty to spare when making all in a 5f handicap at Catterick on his stable debut; 8lb higher and raised two classes but he's unexposed at 5f.
runners.comment: Made all for easy win at Catterick on recent stable debut; 8lb higher but unexposed at 5f

runners.name: Empress Olivia
runners.spotlight: Started the season with two good runs, notably a 6f win at Ripon in April (good to soft); two subsequent runs less convincing and connections now reach for cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Not at her best on last two starts; connections now reach for cheekpieces

runners.name: Yellow Diamonds
runners.spotlight: Ready winner of a four-runner 5f fillies' novice at Musselburgh last October (soft ground; final run for Kevin Philippart de Foy); two runs for new stable have been well short of that level; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Failed to shine in two handicap runs for new yard last month; others look stronger

runners.name: Rotokura Belle
runners.spotlight: Beat Empress Olivia at Catterick in April (5f, good) but unable to match that twice since; steps up in class and needs a career best.
runners.comment: Two 5f handicap wins at a lower level; fairly exposed and needs a personal best

runners.name: Schrodinger's Cat
runners.spotlight: Good second at Thirsk on his stable/seasonal debut in April and backed it up with another good run at Chester last month (both 5f, good); a slow start did him no favours here four weeks ago, unable to adopt his customary prominent position; a 2lb higher mark has now kicked in but a return to form would be no surprise.
runners.comment: Slow start compromised his chance over C&D 4 weeks ago; earlier 2nds read well; can revive

runners.name: Adalida
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 12th attempt, her first 5f start, at Lingfield last month (good ground); while she could have more to offer at this trip, a 7lb rise does ask a much stiffer question of her.
runners.comment: Won well dropped to 5f at Lingfield last month; up 7lb in a much stronger race

runners.name: Angel Numbers
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to her debut win in ten runs since; has shaped well on occasions, however, and no surprise to see her go well with a tongue-tie back in place.
runners.comment: Beaten ten times since winning debut; tongue-tie returns today; should give good account

runners.name: Simba's Pride
runners.spotlight: Two wins and a close second in his three handicap starts, the latest win a snug one at Ripon two weeks ago (6f, good); up 6lb and drops to 5f for the first time since his debut but he's progressive and shouldn't be underestimated.
runners.comment: On the up since handicapping; drops back to 5f but should have more in the tank

runners.name: Straight Ahead
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a pair of AW handicap wins; returned from five months off with a good fourth at Thirsk 12 days ago (5f, good); can win more races but perhaps not this one.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with 2 AW wins; promising reappearance 12 days ago; this may be too competitive

runners.name: Under The Radar
runners.spotlight: Consistent rather than progressive as a 2yo; didn't run badly at Thirsk 12 days ago but more will be needed here and he's due to be 2lb lower from the weekend.
runners.comment: Hasn't progressed after winning a 2yo maiden last May; will be 2lb lower from Saturday

runners.name: Bravo Hotel
runners.spotlight: Finished third in four of his five 2yo starts, all over 5f and on a variety of going; not obviously well treated back from 254 days off.
runners.comment: Finished third in four of his five 2yo runs; not obviously well treated back from a break

runners.name: Ambishio
runners.spotlight: Began the year with two good runs at Southwell but backwards steps in three runs back on turf; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Two good AW runs this year but backwards steps in three turf runs since; others stronger

races.race_name: Event Traffic Control Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Dawn Of Liberation
runners.spotlight: A pound lower than when beating 19 rivals to win this race by 3l last season (good to firm; drawn 2); hasn't been in quite such good form this term, but his best run was when third to Mao Shang Wong at Beverley last time; could go well again but stall 20 far from ideal.
runners.comment: Well drawn when winning this race off 1lb lower in 2025; poorly drawn in 20 this time

runners.name: Mafting
runners.spotlight: Put up 4lb for his Redcar win in April (1m2f, good to firm) but good second off this mark at Doncaster next time (1m4f, good to firm); hasn't done quite as well since; poor deaw.
runners.comment: Put up 4lb for April's Redcar win (1m2f); sound run next time (1m4f) but not so good since

runners.name: Triple Force
runners.spotlight: Went up 20lb for his four wins last season and has found life much tougher this term; fair run last time at Beverley but plenty to find with the winner, Mao Shang Wong.
runners.comment: Four wins last season but well behind Mao Shang Wong when 4th at Beverley last time

runners.name: Mao Shang Wong
runners.spotlight: Three 1m2f wins already this year, the latest at Beverley last month (good to firm); has gone up a total of 11lb but only 3lb higher than last win so could go well despite a tricky draw.
runners.comment: Three 1m2f wins already this year; up 11lb but still feasibly treated and one wth a chance

runners.name: Urban Road
runners.spotlight: Eight of his nine wins have been on the AW, including a hat-trick in February last year; pulled too hard when well beaten last time but had run two sound races ar Redcar prior to that (1m2f, good to firm); worth considering from his inside draw.
runners.comment: Eight of his nine wins have been on AW; two good runs at Redcar this season; a possible
runners.owner: S McMillan, F Reay & A D Brown

runners.name: Narmar
runners.spotlight: Ex-Henry de Bromhead; Ffos Las winner for current yard in June last year (1m, good to soft), but has beaten just two horses home in his two runs this term.
runners.comment: Ffos Las winner last year, but has beaten just two horses home in his two runs this term

runners.name: Beach Point
runners.spotlight: Has done well with cheekpieces, winning twice at 1m2f at Sandown last summer (good/soft); acts on fast ground and following three fair runs this season has an each-way chance.
runners.comment: Two wins with headgear last summer (1m2f, good/soft); fair form this term; each-way chance
runners.owner: John & Peter Seabrook And Tim Fisher

runners.name: Say What You See
runners.spotlight: Has a 5lb penalty for his narrow win at Pontefract on Monday (1m4f, good), having finished runner-up over this trip at Newcastle on his previous start; well-drawn; cheekpieces tried; place chance.
runners.comment: Penalised for narrow win on Monday (1m4f); headgear now tried; should go well again

runners.name: Naples
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns buy in October having won a 1m maiden on heavy ground at Leopardstown for Paddy Twomey in September; fair third stepped up to 1m2f at Chester last time (good to soft); any rain a positive.
runners.comment: 20,000gns buy having won 1m maiden on heavy for Paddy Twomey; fair run latest; needs rain

runners.name: Jez Bomb
runners.spotlight: Won four races from 1m-1m2f last season, including two on fast ground; well beaten over 1m4f here in May, but bounced back with a sound fourth at Carlisle last time (1m, good to firm); on a fair mark now.
runners.comment: Four wins at up to 1m2f last term; solid run when 4th at Carlisle latest; on a good mark

runners.name: Queen Roslyn
runners.spotlight: Won three on the Flat last season, including a 1m4f seller at Beverley in July (1m4f, good to firm); winner over hurdles in March (2m1f, soft) and returns after wind surgery the following month.
runners.comment: Three wins in 2025, including a seller; hurdles win in March, and returns after wind op

runners.name: Dovey Moon
runners.spotlight: Has won two of his last three races, both at around 1m on good ground, the latest at Salisbury last month; tries 1m2f for the first time, is on a career-high mark and has a poor draw, so a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Has won two of last three races, both at around 1m; stamina to prove; on career-high mark

runners.name: Titian
runners.spotlight: Won a 17-runner C&D handicap for amateur riders last June (good) but didn't run as well again; having second run since a wind op but has a wide draw to overcome.
runners.comment: Won 17-runner C&D h'cap last June; second run after wind op but not well drawn

runners.name: Austrian Theory
runners.spotlight: Not the best of strike-rates and not been in top form this year; cheekpieces now tried but has a tricky draw to overcome; well handicapped now.
runners.comment: Not been in top form this season; cheekpieces tried and on a good mark but not well drawn

runners.name: Glistening Nights
runners.spotlight: 1m2f on fast ground suits and comes into the reckoning after two sound placed efforts at Ripon this season, beaten a head into second place last time; chance.
runners.comment: These conditions suit and comes into the reckoning after two sound runs at Ripon this term

runners.name: Epictetus
runners.spotlight: In the frame in eight of his nine starts this year, last time finishing third over 1m1f at Lingfield (good to firm); each-way chance.
runners.comment: In the frame in eight of nine runs this year, latest a third at Lingfield; each-way chance

runners.name: Northwest Passage
runners.spotlight: Only win came at this trip on heavy ground at Salisbury last September when trained by Roger Teal; good second at Newbury the following month (1m2f, soft); changed hands for 31,000gns in February; had wind op in March; yet to hit top form for new connections.
runners.comment: Sole success was on heavy for Roger Teal; 31,000gns buy in February; below-par this year

runners.name: Izzari
runners.spotlight: Has won on turf (1m on good ground at Musselburgh in April last year) but seems better on the AW and has run well below his best on last two starts.
runners.comment: Won on turf last year (1m) but better on the AW; well below his best on last two outings

runners.name: Poet's Dawn
runners.spotlight: Tough veteran who is having his 127th race; two wins already this season, latest at Musselburgh last week (1m1f, good) when he had to pull out all the stops; could go well again from a good draw, particularly if the going eases.
runners.comment: Tough veteran; second win this term was at Musselburgh last week; could go well again

runners.name: Harry The Rogue
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 1m for Andrew Slattery in Ireland last year; beaten nearly 9l over 7f here last time (good); now back up in trip but pulled too hard on only previous try at 1m2f; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Two 1m wins for Andrew Slattery in Ireland; pulled hard previous try at 1m2f; bit to prove

races.race_name: Allington Hughes Law Bicentenary Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (Special Rider Conditions)

runners.name: Kindest Nation
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer but she's not the most reliable of mares and came in last of six in 7f AW Lingfield handicap in December; not ruled out though if back on song after a break in a change of headgear.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; last at Lingfield in December but not ruled out if back on song after a break

runners.name: Lord Capulet
runners.spotlight: Is enjoying a fine season and he racked up a fifth victory at Redcar (7f) in May; posted another very good effort with second of ten in 7f Newcastle handicap 24 days ago so he's a likely player once more.
runners.comment: Very good effort second at Newcastle 24 days ago so he's a likely player once more

runners.name: Spirit Genie
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer who made a promising return from a poor draw when seventh of 22 in 7f York handicap four weeks ago; figures on a handy-looking mark now and he's interesting.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; promising reappearance seventh at York; interesting off a handy-looking mark

runners.name: Mission Command
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2024 but largely in decent nick this season until tried visored when fourth of five at Lingfield (6f) 13 days ago; tongue strap/cheekpieces are refitted here and he's the sort to bounce back.
runners.comment: Winless since 2024 but largely in decent nick this season; possibilities

runners.name: Paws For Thought
runners.spotlight: Course specialist who also took this contest in 2023 (for Lucy Turner); off seven months and not seen to best effect (caught wide) when beating one here (6f) on his return last month; it would come as no surprise to see him play a prominent role now.
runners.comment: Took this in 2023; not seen to best effect on return here so needs considering

runners.name: Moon Beginnings
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who got back on track in first-time cheekpieces with fifth of ten in 1m Carlisle handicap 13 days ago; not out of things with headgear retained.
runners.comment: Back on track with fifth at Carlisle latest; not out of things with headgear retained
runners.owner: Nbr30, Mike Owen & Rasio Cymru Racing

runners.name: Maxi Boy
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2024 but he took a step back in the right direction with fourth of ten in 7f Doncaster handicap last month; needs considering off a 2lb lower mark with Eireann Cagney up.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction with Doncaster fourth latest; considered off a 2lb lower mark

runners.name: King's School
runners.spotlight: Readily landed 7f handicap at Newcastle (fourth success there) 24 days ago; can't be ruled out on a rare turf start despite a 5lb weights rise.
runners.comment: Readily landed 7f handicap at Newcastle 24 days ago; not ruled out on a rare turf start

runners.name: Be Frank
runners.spotlight: A fair and largely reliable 7f winner in 2025; has his first run for nine months here but he has won off a break so is no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Reliable 7f winner in 2025; has first run for nine months here but he has won off a break

runners.name: Hectic
runners.spotlight: Scored at Ripon (6f) last June but yet to hit top form this season, undergoing wind surgery before coming in seventh of ten in 6f Pontefract handicap four days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Scored at Ripon (6f) last June but yet to hit top form this season; others appeal more

runners.name: Calyxoh
runners.spotlight: A fair 7f scorer for Charlie Clover in 2025; little show in a trio of runs in Meydan for Jane Chapple-Hyam early this year though; since changed hands for 10,000gns and he starts out for another new yard with more needed in first-timne cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Little show in a trio of runs in Meydan for Jane Chapple-Hyam; more needed for new yard

races.race_name: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual) (GBB/IRE Incentive)

runners.name: Belardo Ace
runners.spotlight: Belardo colt who built on debut promise with second of ten in 5f Windsor novice 11 days ago; drawn wide here but he can do better still now stepped up to 6f so must still enter calculations.
runners.comment: Built on debut with Windsor second; drawn wide here but must still enter calculations

runners.name: Lady Branksome
runners.spotlight: Has shaped well on both her runs, pulling clear of rest when runner-up in 6f Ripon novice 15 days ago; this Saxon Warrior filly holds leading claims with better likely still to come.
runners.comment: Has shaped well on both runs, clear Ripon second latest; leading claims with more to come

runners.name: Metamouse
runners.spotlight: 33-1, made an encouraging start with fourth of 12 in maiden at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) 22 days ago; this Dubawi Legend colt can build on it here.
runners.comment: Encouraging start with fourth at Chepstow; this Dubawi Legend colt can build on it here

runners.name: Towelontheterrace
runners.spotlight: 62,000 guineas yearling, £10,000 2yo; half-brother to seven winners in Spain, including Abrantes (Group-placed 5f-6f), Mayo (5.5f-8.7f) and Andalucia (6.5f-1m3f); dam French/Spanish 1m1f-1m3f winner (Listed); the market can guide for this Blackbeard colt.
runners.comment: Half-brother to seven winners in Spain but the market can guide for this Blackbeard colt

runners.name: Zambezi Shark
runners.spotlight: 35,000 guineas yearling; Aclaim colt; half-brother to several winners, notably Queen Jo Jo (5f/6f Group 3; RPR 110) and Mr Minerals (7f/1m; RPR 91); dam US 5.5f winner; appeals on paper but the betting can prove an accurate indicator for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Aclaim colt; half-brother to several winners but betting can prove an accurate indicator
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Harry's Pop
runners.spotlight: 11-1 and green, encouraging debut seventh of 12 in maiden at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 20 days ago; this son of Harry Angel can take a step forward.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut seventh at Windsor; this son of Harry Angel can take a step forward
runners.owner: K Shenton, N Bradley 6 & D Cunha

runners.name: Koodini
runners.spotlight: Well held on both his starts here last month but he's suffered a poor run each time, poorly drawn in C&D novice 13 days ago; in good hands and can take a step forward now.
runners.comment: Well held both runs here but suffered a poor run each time; can take a step forward now

runners.name: Trust In Far
runners.spotlight: Blackbeard filly who offered little in 6f Haydock maiden and seller over C&D last month; significantly more is needed.
runners.comment: Offered little in Haydock maiden and C&D seller last month; significantly more is needed

runners.name: Trust Havana
runners.spotlight: 80-1 and green, always behind when tenth of 11 in C&D novice (good) on her first run 13 days ago; lots more is required from this Havana Grey filly.
runners.comment: Debut tenth of 11 over C&D 13 days ago; lots more required from this Havana Grey filly

races.race_name: Danny And Harriett Birthday Bash Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Morbeh
runners.spotlight: Very much on the up since sent handicapping and he cosily bagged a second success at Windsor (11.5f) last month; up 4lb but that form is working out really well so this son of Dubawi is a big player.
runners.comment: Very much on up and cosy Windsor scorer latest; up 4lb but form franked so a big player

runners.name: Silver State
runners.spotlight: Is enjoying a fine season and racked up a fourth victory at Doncaster (1m) in April; posted another very good effort when runner-up in 1m2f handicap at Epsom six days ago; this likeable front runner is a player once more off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Very good runner-up at Epsom six days ago; this likeable front runner is a player again

runners.name: Magic Trip
runners.spotlight: Posted a solid fifth on his handicap debut at Sandown (1m2f) in April but failed to beat a rival in 1m2f York handicap following month; visor is reached for now.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in 1m2f York handicap following month; visor is reached for now

runners.name: Home Hero
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each of his four runs to date, resuming from nine months off with a very good third on his handicap debut over C&D 13 days ago; well in the mix off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Resumed with a very good C&D third 13 days ago; well in the mix off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Mr Seagull
runners.spotlight: Winless since his debut but (cheekpieces on) he has advanced his form when fourth in handicaps at Haydock (1m) and York (1m2f) this spring; holds solid claims eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Advanced his form when fourth at Haydock and York this spring; solid claims eased 1lb
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Asia Force
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when runner-up on all his three starts this season, latest in 1m2f Pontefract novice in April; this Frankel colt is open to progress now stepping into handicap company.
runners.comment: Shaped well when runner-up all three starts; open to progress on handicap debut

runners.name: Simon Pierre
runners.spotlight: Zelzal colt who was much improved from his debut when landing 1m Thirsk novice in April; strong at the finish there so capable of better still now up in trip for his handicap debut; not without interest.
runners.comment: Landed Thirsk novice in April; capable of better still up in trip for his handicap debut

runners.name: Colourband
runners.spotlight: Built on earlier promise to land 1m2f Bath novice on his return but came in last of six on his handicap debut at Sandown (1m2f) later in April; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Last of six on his handicap debut at Sandown (1m2f) later in April; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Valpovo
runners.spotlight: Son of Zarak who showed fair form in three runs in 2025, landing 9.5f minor event at La Teste De Buch in September; since changed hands for 40,000gns but this looks no easy ask for his new yard on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Fair ex-French winner; this looks no easy ask for new yard on his handicap debut though

races.race_name: Allington Hughes Law Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Crazee Icon
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but she was runner-up for the third time at Windsor (1m) in May and then suffered a very poor run when beaten just over 2l into fifth in 1m Newmarket handicap four weeks ago; merits serious consideration despite 2lb weights rise.
runners.comment: Suffered a very poor run when fifth at Newmarket latest; merits serious consideration

runners.name: Vidmiyr
runners.spotlight: On the up since joining current yard, landing 7f Lingfield maiden in May before posting an excellent second of six in 7.5f handicap there 13 days ago; up 3lb but he's a likely player again.
runners.comment: Excellent second at Lingfield 13 days ago; up 3lb but he's a likely player again
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Cool Molly
runners.spotlight: A four-time AW winner (from 5f to 7f) this year and ran really well when third over C&D in May. Proved too free when well held in big-field Goodwood handicap (7f) last time and very much the sort to bounce back returned here.
runners.comment: Too free when well held at Goodwood last time; very much the sort to bounce back here

runners.name: Norman Invasion
runners.spotlight: Son of Lope De Vega who has shaped with promise on all his three runs, proving more settled (gelded over winter) on his recent return when third of ten in 1m maiden at Redcar; has more to offer now stepping into handicap company in a refitted hood; interesting.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise with Redcar third on return; more to offer on h'cap debut; interesting

runners.name: Dartrey
runners.spotlight: Rattled off a Southwell hat-trick (6f) in January but he failed to beat a rival in 1m Windsor handicap last month on his return from a four-month absence; he needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Rattled off hat-trick at Southwell in January but failed to beat a rival at Windsor latest

runners.name: Jackabi
runners.spotlight: A fair 7f juvenile maiden but he went too freely in a refitted hood when only 12th in 7f Salisbury handicap on his recent reappearance; tongue tie is added now.
runners.comment: Too free when 12th in Salisbury handicap on recent reappearance; tongue tie is added now

runners.name: Ubetterseethis
runners.spotlight: Course scorer at 2yrs; returned with a good third at Beverley (7.5f) in April but beat only one in 20-runner handicap at York (7f) 20 days ago; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Course scorer; beat only one at York (7f) 20 days ago; needs to get back on track

runners.name: Showcasing Star
runners.spotlight: Son of Showcasing who has finished runner-up on all his three starts this spring, advancing his form in 1m Newcastle maiden in April; back from a break for his handicap debut now and needs considering.
runners.comment: Runner-up all three starts, in 1m Newcastle maiden in April; considered on his h'cap debut

runners.name: Runswick
runners.spotlight: A fair 7f winner at 2yrs; resumed from eight months off with a very good third of nine on his handicap debut at Nottingham (1m) 24 days ago, leading long way; this Ghaiyyath colt must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Very good third at Nottingham 24 days ago; this Ghaiyyath colt must enter calculations

races.race_name: Allington Hughes Law Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Miss Lady Grace
runners.spotlight: Much improved for the switch to Dan Horsford this season, gaining a second success in fine style in 7f Goodwood apprentice handicap a week ago; escapes a penalty here so she looks hard to beat with Alexandra Egan again up.
runners.comment: Scored in fine style at Goodwood (apprentice) week ago; escapes penalty; the one to beat
runners.owner: R H J Martin And Mrs J Martin

runners.name: Abundant
runners.spotlight: Progressive at 2yrs when 1m Southwell maiden winner in October; resumed from nearly 7 months off (gelded) with a solid fourth of 12 in 1m Windsor handicap last month; can take a step forward here.
runners.comment: Resumed with solid fourth of 12 in Windsor h'cap last month; can take a step forward here

runners.name: Pints In Peace
runners.spotlight: Made a bright start to life for Ivan Furtado on the AW this season, bagging a pair of 7f Wolverhampton handicaps; not so good back on turf with 15th in 7f York handicap so needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Won twice on AW for Ivan Furtado this season but only 15th at York last time; more needed

runners.name: The Resdev Scholar
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick at Doncaster (6f) in November and continued in very good form when placed in four handicaps this season, including when third of 14 at York (7f) in May. Came in well held there (7f) last time but he's the sort to bounce back.
runners.comment: In very good form this season until well held at York latest; sort to bounce back though
runners.owner: N Bradley 29, Resdev & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Lucky Hero
runners.spotlight: Gained a first victory in 7f Redcar novice on his return in April but he came in a well-held 17th in 7f York handicap 20 days ago; cheekpieces go on for the first time now.
runners.comment: Won at Redcar but well-held 17th at York 20 days ago; cheekpieces go on for the first time

runners.name: Berkshire Boom
runners.spotlight: Landed 7f maiden here last July; off ten months and has failed to kick on sent handicapping this season, again going freely when last of six at Lingfield (7.5f) 13 days ago; could still do better if becoming more tractable.
runners.comment: Too free when last of six at Lingfield latest; could still do better if more tractable
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Gizmo
runners.spotlight: Landed 7f novice at Lingfield on his debut in September but he has failed to beat a rival in both subsequent starts, going freely when last of six on his handicap debut at Newmarket (7f) four weeks ago. Hood is now reached for.
runners.comment: Debut winner at Lingfield but he has failed to beat a rival in both subsequent starts

runners.name: Sprinting Speed
runners.spotlight: Hinted at promise in a trio of runs for Richard Hannon in 2025 and it's been a similar story this season for his new yard, hampered start when eighth of ten in 7f Wolverhampton handicap 25 days ago; no forlorn hope off a 3lb lower mark here.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise; hampered start when eighth at Wolverhampton latest; no forlorn hope

runners.name: Master Of Entropy
runners.spotlight: Has progressed with each of his three runs, fifth of ten in 1m Redcar novice last month; since gelded and this son of Mohaather could have more to offer now going handicapping; very much one to consider.
runners.comment: Progressive; gelded before Redcar fith last month; could have more to offer on h'cap debut

races.race_name: Chester Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Star Cast
runners.spotlight: Is enjoying a fine season and she bagged a fourth success at Ripon (1m2f) in May; posted another good effort when beaten a short head in 1m1f Carlisle handicap four days ago; well in the mix again off the same mark.
runners.comment: Fourth win at Ripon in May; beaten short head at Carlisle four days ago so a likely player

runners.name: Tryst
runners.spotlight: A fair 8.5f juvenile winner; made a bright start to handicapping with Wolverhampton runner-up in April and best forgiven her Ascot (1m2f) seventh last month when suffering a poor run; she remains with potential and is a player here.
runners.comment: Best forgiven Ascot seventh last month when suffering a poor run; remains with potential

runners.name: Winged One
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced Australia filly who got off the mark at the second attempt in 1m Newcastle novice in October; not disgraced when last of five in Cheshire Oaks here on her return but she finished well held in 1m2f Goodwood Listed event 20 days ago; needs to bounce back now going handicapping.
runners.comment: Well held in Goodwood Listed event 20 days ago; needs to bounce back now handicapping

runners.name: Princess Rascal
runners.spotlight: A two-time 1m2f scorer in 2025; off six months and not disgraced despite coming in last of four in 1m2f Salisbury handicap on her return last month; no forlorn hope off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Not disgraced despite coming last of four at Salisbury on her return; no forlorn hope

runners.name: Patagonia Girl
runners.spotlight: Looked on the up when making all over 1m4f here in May; found a Group 3 at Carlisle (1m3f) 13 days ago too tough an ask but this C&D scorer can bounce back returned to handicap company now.
runners.comment: Won here in May but stiff task in Carlisle Group 3 since; this C&D scorer can bounce back

runners.name: Lady Ridgewood
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Peter Chapple-Hyam with a breakthrough win at Chelsmsford (1m2f) in August; off eight months and has failed to beat a rival in handicaps at Southwell (1m3f) and Ascot (1m2f) this spring though.
runners.comment: Has failed to beat a rival in handicaps at Southwell (1m3f) and Ascot (1m2f) this spring

runners.name: Model Yuko
runners.spotlight: Readily gained a first win in 8.5f Wolverhampton maiden and looked on the up until (hooded) beating only one in 1m2f Listed event at Longchamp in September; this Al Kazeem filly remains low mileage and is well worth another chance back in handicap company for her seasonal return.
runners.comment: On the up until beating one in Longchamp Listed event in September; worth another chance

runners.name: Quebella
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful and consistent for Richard Hughes in 2025; made an encouraging start for her new yard after eight months off with third of nine in 1m4f Goodwood handicap three weeks ago, keeping on late; should strip sharper here and not out of things.
runners.comment: Encouraging start for new yard with Goodwood third; should strip sharper here

races.race_name: Allington Hughes 200 Year Anniversary Celebrations Handicap

runners.name: Dolos Dream
runners.spotlight: The winner of two of her four starts, the latest a 5f handicap at Pontefract ten days ago (made all); that effort is backed up by the Topspeed figure but she is 2lb badly in under a 6lb penalty; promise over 6f on debut; still low mileage.
runners.comment: 2-4, both wins at 5f; 2lb wrong under her penalty but she still has time to do better

runners.name: Explosive Finnish
runners.spotlight: More than capable of winning a similar race judged on his three handicap runs this year, latterly a second of 12 in a Class 4 at Thirsk 12 days ago (5f, good; third-placed horse has won since); handles good to soft but untried on slower; solid contender.
runners.comment: Placed in all three handicaps this year; latest Class 4 second already franked; contender

runners.name: Saucy Jane
runners.spotlight: Came to hand early as a 2yo and her progress has stagnated; did run well in first-time cheekpieces at Pontefract ten days ago (6f, good) but she will need to up her game to come out on top.
runners.comment: Back to form tried in cheekpieces ten days ago (6f, good); she'll need more to take this
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 16 And Jmr Partners

runners.name: Nebulon
runners.spotlight: Promise in all three starts this year, most recently when second of eight on his handicap debut at Newmarket (6f, good) four weeks ago; tongue tied first time today; not fully exposed.
runners.comment: Second of eight on handicap debut last month; now tongue tied; unexposed

runners.name: Tactical Blitz
runners.spotlight: Confirmed his maiden/novice promise when making a winning handicap debut in March (6f, AW); no improvement for 7f in two subsequent runs and sold out of Archie Watson's stable for 11,000gns in May; still not fully exposed; turf debut.
runners.comment: 6f AW win for Archie Watson; worth a market check on his stable debut

runners.name: Try Storm Cat
runners.spotlight: 6f win as a 2yo for Charlie Johnston; well beaten in three 7f handicaps for his new stable; dropping down the weights but rain wouldn't be ideal; cheekpieces back on today.
runners.comment: Yet to shine for current yard, albeit over 7f; wouldn't want rain; cheekpieces return

runners.name: Start Me Up
runners.spotlight: Gelded since failing to live up to his market billing at Southwell in March; easing down the weights and this is a drop in class so not discounted; tongue tie worn on his last two starts is now dispensed with.
runners.comment: Drops in class having been gelded; could still have a bigger effort in him

runners.name: Trust Amy
runners.spotlight: C&D win last autumn (good to soft) and then ran well in a Doncaster Listed event on heavy going; well held in her two runs this year but now drops in class.
runners.comment: C&D winner; yet to shine in two runs this year but this is a drop in class

races.race_name: Tom Jones At Chester Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Dapper Gee Gee
runners.spotlight: Landed his third win at Chepstow (7f, good to soft) last month and ran up to that form when runner-up back there (soft) six days ago; still on a workable mark and holds claims.
runners.comment: Landed his third win at Chepstow last month and was runner-up back there six days ago.

runners.name: Oman
runners.spotlight: Of his six wins on the Flat, two have been at this track over 1m4f+; returned to winning ways with Myla Coppins aboard at Goodwood (1m1f, good to soft) last Friday and escapes a penalty as it was a race for apprentices; hasn't raced over a distance this short since his debut six years ago and vulnerable to speedier sorts.
runners.comment: Returned to winning ways at Goodwood last Friday; unraced over trip this short since debut

runners.name: Military Leader
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced, he landed a quickfire double at Southwell and Thirsk over 1m last month and was beaten 2l when third of 12 at Newcastle (1m, AW) ten days ago; there may be more to come and he should not be far away.
runners.comment: Lightly raced, he landed a quickfire double at Southwell and Thirsk over 1m last month.

runners.name: Born A Rebel
runners.spotlight: 3-15 on turf; beaten 1l in 16-runner handicap at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) when last seen in April; would hold claims if she can build on that effort.
runners.comment: Beaten 1l in 16-runner handicap at Doncaster when last seen in April.

runners.name: Mariner
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent sort who followed up a neck second at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) in April with a mediocre effort when eighth of 12 at Redcar (1m, good) last month; cannot discount but needs to put his best foot forward.
runners.comment: Followed up a neck second at Doncaster in April with an eighth at Redcar last month.

runners.name: Red Mirage
runners.spotlight: Has a win over C&D but that was over four years ago; hasn't backed up an encouraging third at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) in March in two runs since but has dropped to a career-low mark so all is not lost.
runners.comment: Hasn't backed up an encouraging third at Doncaster in March in two runs since.

runners.name: Coolree
runners.spotlight: Has been in excellent form this year, winning twice at his beloved Beverley (four course wins there); it will be interesting if he can maintain his form here, but he will certainly be worth looking out for when returning to his favourite track.
runners.comment: Has been in excellent form this year, winning twice at his beloved Beverley.

runners.name: Pension Pot
runners.spotlight: Faded into 3l fifth of nine on handicap/seasonal debut at Nottingham in April and this headstrong sort also looked stretched by 1m at Thirsk last time; the return to shorter looks the right move for this lightly raced 4yo, who is a possible player.
runners.comment: Headstrong sort who looked stretched by 1m at Thirsk last time.

runners.name: Nordic Norm
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since his debut in February last year when trained by Karl Burke; went close when beaten a nose at Chelmsford (1m, AW) in March and if he can reproduce that effort on turf then he would be a player.
runners.comment: Went close when beaten a nose at Chelmsford in March.

runners.name: Not Me
runners.spotlight: Unplaced on all five starts on turf; has switched stables since his last run at Beverley last month and hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Unplaced on all five starts on turf; has switched stables since his last run.

runners.name: Chilly Breeze
runners.spotlight: Early-season 5f winner as a 2yo when trained by David Evans; unplaced in two handicap runs last summer; beaten 10l on stable debut at Yarmouth last month; best watched.
runners.comment: Beaten 10l on stable debut at Yarmouth last month; best watched.

races.race_name: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Viking Barbie
runners.spotlight: Left debut form behind to finish third at Salisbury (5f, good), though was no match for the front two after starting slowly; looks vulnerable to anything with proper potential but has the benefit of two races under her belt and Oisin Murphy takes over.
runners.comment: Has the benefit of two races under her belt and Oisin Murphy takes over

runners.name: Mysterious Times
runners.spotlight: 425,000gns yearling; second foal; brother to 6f winner Maelstrom (RPR 88); dam 6f 2yo winner (Group 3; 105), closely related to 7f Group 3 winner Esquire; stable's winner of this last year had the benefit of a run, but this colt looks the part on paper and could be smart enough to overcome his wide draw.
runners.comment: This colt looks the part on paper and could be smart enough to overcome his wide draw

runners.name: Havana Grey Star
runners.spotlight: Shown promise at 6f/5f without looking entirely straightforward under pressure; might be learning on the job and the draw is favourable.
runners.comment: Shown promise at 6f/5f without looking entirely straightforward under pressure

runners.name: Perigord Noir
runners.spotlight: Similar sort of form in 6f races at Kempton and Windsor; probably has to take a step forward, but he looks quick enough for 5f and has the inside stall.
runners.comment: Two okay runs over 6f; he looks quick enough for 5f and has the inside stall

runners.name: Princess Ivy
runners.spotlight: 27,000gns yearling; second foal; dam unraced sister to 2m3f hurdle winner Crossbeau, half-sister to winners Majestic Dawn (1m2f Listed) and Rousing Encore (Group-placed 5f/6f); must be showing speed at home to be kicking off over the minimum trip.
runners.comment: 27,000gns yearling; must be showing speed at home to be kicking off over the minimum trip

runners.name: Henley On Thames
runners.spotlight: Made 17,000euros as a foal, 22,000euros as a yearling and £125,000 at the breeze-ups; dam unraced sister to French 12.5f/2m winner Cosmic Front, out of unraced sister to 1m5f/2m Listed winner Aloft and half-sister to 6f Group 3 winner Beauty Bright; the market should tell a tale as to expectations.
runners.comment: Made 17,000euros as a foal, 22,000euros as a yearling and £125,000 at the breeze-ups

runners.name: Gold Reef City
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Egoli (RPR 90); dam Listed-placed 1m winner (103), half-sister to winners Havana Gold (1m Group 1) and Rocker (prolific at 5f); stable's 2yos are going well and has to be of strong interest.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Egoli (RPR 90); stable's 2yos are going well

runners.name: Igrok
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns foal, 45,000gns yearling; third foal; half-brother to 5f/6f 2yo winner Argentine Tango (RPR 101); dam unplaced 5f/6f 2yo (54), half-sister to winners Hearts Of Fire (Italian 1m 2yo Group 1) and Kenny Powers (Listed-placed 7f); would need to see some confidence behind him.
runners.comment: 10,000gns foal, 45,000gns yearling; would need to see some confidence behind him

runners.name: Underdog
runners.spotlight: £140,000 yearling; tenth foal; half-brother to six winners including Well Done Fox (5f 2yo; RPR 107), Excellently Poised (5f 2yo; 83), High Security (5f; 77), Lucius Aurelius (5f; 72) and Noelies Dream (5f 2yo; 65); dam 5f winner (105); bred to be useful and sharp; needs checking in the betting.
runners.comment: £140,000 yearling; bred to be useful and sharp; needs checking in the betting

runners.name: Courseyoudo
runners.spotlight: Dropped out at York and was 80-1 when well behind Viking Barbie at Salisbury; nurseries beckon.
runners.comment: Dropped out at York and was 80-1 when well behind Viking Barbie at Salisbury
runners.owner: Fairies At The Bottom Of The Garden Ltd

races.race_name: Chris Barnett Memorial Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Over Spiced
runners.spotlight: Consistent filly who has been running well and this mark isn't beyond her; goes on all ground and Oisin Murphy rides; solid.
runners.comment: Consistent filly who has been running well and this mark isn't beyond her

runners.name: Silver Wraith
runners.spotlight: Bidding for a Bath hat-trick when finding one too good three weeks ago and might be vulnerable off the same mark from her wide draw; does go on any ground.
runners.comment: Beaten at Bath last time and might be vulnerable off the same mark from her wide draw

runners.name: Sandscreendeliverd
runners.spotlight: Only 1-14 but he's a strong traveller who only narrowly missed out at Lingfield (6f) and Goodwood (5f, good) before a midfield finish at Windsor where never really involved; has a big effort in him.
runners.comment: Two near misses before never involved last time at Windsor; has a big effort in him

runners.name: Rage Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Only 1-15 and seems to have some quirks; quiet on two visits to Ascot in May and probably wants further than this as well.
runners.comment: Quiet on two visits to Ascot in May and probably wants further than this as well

runners.name: Charlie Mason
runners.spotlight: Consistent 5yo who came clear with the second when justifying market support last weekend at Lingfield (6f, soft); carries a 5lb penalty and Myla Coppins claimed 7lb on Saturday, so these terms are tougher; still a likely contender and any rain welcomed.
runners.comment: Ready winner last weekend but these terms tougher without a 7lb claimer; still respected

runners.name: Good Earth
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner but went up 6lb for his 2l win at Haydock and that counted when fifth at Catterick; however, he does have a very solid record here.
runners.comment: Went up 6lb for his 2l win at Haydock and that counted when fifth at Catterick

runners.name: Tan Rapido
runners.spotlight: Beneath his last winning mark (5f AW) and his second run back from a break was more encouraging, as although only eighth at Kempton he wasn't beaten too far (6f) from an unpromising position; capable of racing prominently, which would be helpful from stall 1, and could be interesting; soft ground would be a concern.
runners.comment: Latest run was encouraging for all that he came eighth; good mark and well drawn

runners.name: Lipsink
runners.spotlight: Has won four of his last seven starts and the latest defeat at Chelmsford was no backward step; however, his mark has taken a hit and this 9yo is returning from a lengthy break; fine on all ground.
runners.comment: In form when last seen but his mark took a hit and he's returning from a lengthy break

runners.name: Twilight Calls
runners.spotlight: Over four years since his last win and made only limited impact at Meydan earlier this year before a midfield finish on his belated AW debut; however, he's on a very dangerous mark should the visor trigger something; best on good or faster ground so watch the weather.
runners.comment: Ages since he won but on a very dangerous mark should the visor trigger something

races.race_name: Chris Barnett Memorial Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Betties Bay
runners.spotlight: Only narrowly failed when bidding for a five-timer last week at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) and she's 3lb well-in on the back of that effort; whether she wants the ground slower than good is questionable, and her improvement has come at 6f.
runners.comment: In-form mare who is 3lb well-in but this softer surface casts some doubts

runners.name: Cayman Tai
runners.spotlight: Back in good form off his reduced mark, winning easily at Southwell (5f) before nearly defying a penalty at Chepstow (good to firm; effective on softer); 3lb higher for this but well drawn and must have a shout for all that he's back up in grade; Seamus Cronin aboard for last three races.
runners.comment: Back in good form off his reduced mark; 3lb higher than for recent close second

runners.name: Kiss And Run
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but closely matched with Lequinto on their tight encounter at Windsor last time (5f, good) when she might have won but for hanging left late on; she doesn't always do that and has to be feared.
runners.comment: On a losing run but closely matched with Lequinto on their tight encounter at Windsor

runners.name: Carbine Harvester
runners.spotlight: Both wins and best form has been on the AW over 6f; third over that far at Yarmouth last time but was comfortably held; back at 5f from a high draw, he looks up against it.
runners.comment: Both wins and best form has been on the AW over 6f; beaten about 7l last time at Yarmouth

runners.name: Marching Mac
runners.spotlight: Multiple sprint winner who coped well with the rise in grade (Class 2) at Windsor on Monday and gets in here off a 3lb lower mark; that was from off the pace but can race prominently (drawn in 1); good chance.
runners.comment: 3lb lower than when a close third in a higher grade at Windsor on Monday

runners.name: Em Four
runners.spotlight: Has won over 6f and 5f on the AW since March and he's perfectly capable on turf, though perhaps not on ground easier than good judged on latest run at Chepstow.
runners.comment: Six-time winner; below par on good to soft last time and that's worrying

runners.name: Lequinto
runners.spotlight: Loves Windsor where he beat Kiss And Run before finishing third at Bath; has won here but usually runs in Class 5s.
runners.comment: Loves Windsor but has won here; however, he usually runs in Class 5s

runners.name: Fantasy Master
runners.spotlight: Soon off the mark for this yard at Chelmsford (AW) in December and it was a fine return when quickly out of the stalls and finishing third of 18 at Ascot (again 6f, good to firm); respected off the same mark despite his unfavourable draw; goes on all ground.
runners.comment: Third of 18 at Ascot last time and respected off same mark despite his unfavourable draw

runners.name: Nogo's Dream
runners.spotlight: Has registered two wins apiece on AW and turf; back from a break with a near miss at Nottingham and a length defeat at Goodwood, again over 5f on good ground; should run well again from stall 4; has form on all sorts of surfaces.
runners.comment: Back from a break with two competitive shows on turf; thereabouts from stall 4
