races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pat's Cash
runners.spotlight: Oisin Murphy was also aboard at Haydock (7f, good to soft) where he kept at it off a modest pace to bustle up the winner who went on to be fourth in last week's Woodcote at Epsom; this colt is clearly useful and a tongue-tie is added; much respected with experience under his belt.
runners.comment: Close second at Haydock and the winner was fourth in last week's Woodcote at Epsom

runners.name: King's Welcome
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt who was bought for 1,900,000gns at the Tattersalls October Book 1 sale; half-brother to winners Topanga (7f; RPR 99) and Time Allowed (1m; 87); dam runner-up 7f/1m (76), out of 1m Group 1 winning half-sister to 1m2f Group 1 winner Passage Of Time; intriguing newcomer from a yard that won this with Saba Desert last year and Native Trial in 2021, both newcomers.
runners.comment: Wootton Bassett colt who was bought for 1,900,000gns; intriguing newcomer

runners.name: Il Capo
runners.spotlight: 115,000gns yearling; second foal; half-brother to 5f/6f 2yo winner Kairyu (Group 3; RPR 109); dam runner-up at 1m (71), sister to 1m4f/12.4f winner Emaraty Hero; pedigree a mix of speed and stamina; the betting can guide as regards expectations.
runners.comment: 115,000gns yearling; 2nd foal; half-brother to 5f/6f 2yo winner Kairyu (Group 3; RPR 109)

runners.name: Encounter
runners.spotlight: Only 9-4 with Ryan Moore booked when no match for the favourite at Lingfield (7f, good); that was a positive start but he faces some interesting newcomers here.
runners.comment: Only 9-4 with Ryan Moore booked when no match for the favourite at Lingfield (7f)

runners.name: Gymbaazy
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros yearling; third foal; brother to 1m winner Bomberg (RPR 91); dam French 10.5f/1m4f winner (Group 3; 105), half-sister to Listed-placed 1m3f winner Podemos; market useful.
runners.comment: 20,000euros yearling; third foal; brother to 1m winner Bomberg (RPR 91)

runners.name: Kaito
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; brother to 7f 2yo winner Resolute Man (RPR 89), closely related to smart 7f-1m2f performer Boiling Point and Asuka (6f 2yo; 90); dam placed at 1m (74), half-sister to 1m4f Group 3 winner Stellar Mass; another well-bred newcomer who would command utmost respect if at all strong in the market.
runners.comment: Fifth foal; brother to 7f 2yo winner Resolute Man (RPR 89); respected connections
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Wise Warrior
runners.spotlight: 35,000gns foal, 50,000gns yearling; seventh foal; brother to Listed-placed 7f AW winner Padraig Dawn (RPR 103), half-brother to 7f winner Like A Lion (82); dam 7f 2yo winner (Group 1; 110); bred to be useful but probably kicking off in a very good race.
runners.comment: 50,000gns yearling; bred to be useful but probably kicking off in a very good race

races.race_name: Molson Coors Handicap

runners.name: Dangerman
runners.spotlight: Got his act together last season once the blinkers went on, going back-to-back over C&D (good) before finishing third behind the well-handicapped Saddadd (third in a Group 1 this season) on his return visit (soft); interesting on all that as he's still on a fair mark, and perhaps Epsom didn't float his boat when reappearing in April.
runners.comment: Big player on last season's form here; perhaps resented Epsom on comeback

runners.name: Alcarath
runners.spotlight: Impressed in a 1m2f novice last season and hasn't been disgraced in any of his three handicaps, especially last time at Newbury (1m2f, good) where he was never ideally positioned; possibilities.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and never ideally positioned when fourth in a good race at Newbury latest

runners.name: Greek Order
runners.spotlight: Has some back-class but now 0-9 for this yard after beating one home on his return to 1m2f at Windsor 18 days ago; on the plus side, this bigger field might be more to his liking and in two previous visits to Sandown he's finished first and third; no surprise if he went well under Oisin Murphy, under whom he was fourth in last year's Golden Mile at Glorious Goodwood (on soft).
runners.comment: 0-9 for this yard but retains plenty of ability and he has a good record here

runners.name: Galyx
runners.spotlight: Down the field at York a month ago after 322 days off but that was on good ground and his best form has come on soft and heavy; handicapped to go well should conditions turn in his favour.
runners.comment: Handicapped to go well should conditions turn in his favour (more rain the better)

runners.name: Foreseen
runners.spotlight: Made all up the favoured stands' rail over Lingfield's extended 7f a month ago but has only gone up 3lb and he's likeably consistent; this does look more competitive than usual, however.
runners.comment: Made all up the favoured stands' rail over Lingfield's extended 7f a month ago

runners.name: Mythical Bird
runners.spotlight: Emphatic winner on her reappearance at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in April and the 8lb rise probably a factor in her coming up short at Leicester (good to firm); this is tougher again.
runners.comment: AW winner; third off this mark at Leicester and this looks tougher again

runners.name: Carnival Day
runners.spotlight: All wins have been at 1m2f, on ground ranging from soft to good; therefore, his reappearance effort when a closing third (just behind Sweet Reward) over the extended 1m at Epsom can be viewed positively.
runners.comment: Closing third over an extended 1m on comeback; all three wins have come over this far

runners.name: Boyfriend
runners.spotlight: Usually gives his running and won with a bit in hand over 1m here two weeks ago (1m, good); goes further this afternoon and off a career-high mark (4lb higher), so likely to need more.
runners.comment: Recent course winner over 1m; further to go now and up to a career-high mark

runners.name: Whitcombe Rockstar
runners.spotlight: Fine from 1m to 1m4f; has won on turf but the AW looks more his scene and especially Kempton where he beat all bar a handicap snip the last time; back on grass others look safer.
runners.comment: Has won on turf but the AW looks more his scene and especially Kempton

runners.name: Tipsy Tiger
runners.spotlight: Last successful over C&D on soft ground in September 2024; reproduced that best form on two occasions during a light campaign in 2025 and rain-softened ground would be a major plus for him.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2024; two good efforts last year in a light campaign; rain welcomed

runners.name: Baltic Voyage
runners.spotlight: 6yo who is 0-5 over hurdles and 1-13 on the Flat; tailed off in this year's two runs, at 1m2f and 1m.
runners.comment: 0-5 over hurdles and 1-13 on the Flat; tailed off in this year's two runs, at 1m2f and 1m

runners.name: Mustazeed
runners.spotlight: Very capable off this mark when on his game but he refused to race on final run of last season and hasn't looked overly keen to get involved this campaign.
runners.comment: Very capable off this mark but the starts have increasingly become an issue

runners.name: Samuel Colt
runners.spotlight: 0-7 since his winning debut at two for Aidan O'Brien; has contested strong Class 2 handicaps since being gelded last summer (hooded) but was beaten about 10l on both occasions; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Debut winner for Aidan O'Brien but 0-7 since; beaten about 10l in last two races

runners.name: Al Aali
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in France (9.5f, AW) as a 2yo; early days with George Scott and latest Newbury run (1m2f, good) wasn't devoid of promise for all that he never threatened; could come good again at some point.
runners.comment: Latest Newbury run (1m2f, good) wasn't devoid of promise for all that he never threatened

runners.name: Sweet Reward
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2021 and was third in one of the divisions 12 months ago; returns off a 6lb higher mark than last year but on the back of two wins at Lingfield, the latest narrowly from the front off 3lb lower; thriving 9yo who must have some sort of chance.
runners.comment: Bidding for a hat-trick; won this in 2021 and was third in a division last year

races.race_name: Ministry Of Sound At Sandown Park 24 July Handicap

runners.name: Dryburgh
runners.spotlight: 0-10, but came closest to winning when beaten half a length at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) a week ago (handles good to soft); 4lb well in, but meets some less exposed rivals.
runners.comment: Beaten half a length at Doncaster a week ago; 4lb well in, but meets less exposed rivals

runners.name: Al Maslool
runners.spotlight: Some promise in his first two starts on the AW, but hasn't really progressed in two starts on turf; bred to be better than that and interesting to see how he copes in first-time blinkers having been gelded since last seen.
runners.comment: Some promise in two starts on the AW, but hasn't progressed on turf; gelded/blinkers on

runners.name: Brighton View
runners.spotlight: Improved for the switch to a handicap and the longer trip when winning by a neck at Newbury (1m2f, good) four weeks ago (third has won since); a player following a 2lb rise.
runners.comment: Won by a neck on handicap debut at Newbury last month; a player following a 2lb rise

runners.name: Bridge Of Eagles
runners.spotlight: Narrowly made a successful reappearance/handicap debut over C&D (good) early last month, but proved a disappointing favourite at York (1m2f, good) last time; needs to bounce back, but the return to Sandown may help.
runners.comment: Disappointing at York after winning over C&D on reappearance; return to Sandown may help
runners.owner: C & L Washbourn And Mrs S Woodward

runners.name: Akho Mezzna
runners.spotlight: Consistent as a 2yo, winning a novice at Salisbury in October (7f, good), but having been gelded he has been held in two 1m handicaps since returning in April; more needed upped in trip, though Oisin Murphy is 1-1 on him.
runners.comment: Held in two 1m handicaps since returning in April; Oisin Murphy is 1-1 on him

runners.name: Plan C
runners.spotlight: Showed some fair form in three starts over 7f/1m as a 2yo, but comfortably held on handicap debut at York (1m2f, good) last month; may have needed the run after 248 days off, so too early to be giving up on him.
runners.comment: Showed some fair form in three starts as a 2yo and may have needed last month's return

runners.name: Devil's Peak
runners.spotlight: In the frame in his last six starts, but was disappointing when fourth of five at Ascot (1m2f, good to firm) last month; may not have appreciated the fast ground, but still has something to prove; blinkers given a go.
runners.comment: In the frame in his last six starts, but was disappointing last time; blinkers on

runners.name: Tambora
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in three starts over 7f-1m2f at Newbury; open to progress now handicapping but whether he wants this far is debatable.
runners.comment: Open to progress now handicapping but whether he wants this far is debatable

runners.name: Chapter
runners.spotlight: Narrowly made a successful nursery debut at Salisbury (7f, heavy) last September and ran well on similar ground at Doncaster (7f) the following month, but having been gelded he was well held on last month's Nottingham reappearance (1m, good to soft); blinkers given a go, but not certain to appreciate the longer trip.
runners.comment: Well held on last month's reappearance; not certain to appreciate this trip; blinkers on

runners.name: Edwin Hubble
runners.spotlight: Placed in his last three starts last season, the latest when beaten 5l into second of seven here (1m1f, good) in October; market useful now making his handicap debut after eight months off.
runners.comment: Placed in last three starts; market useful on handicap debut after eight months off

runners.name: Ohara
runners.spotlight: No show on last month's Windsor reappearance (1m, good to firm), but relished the conditions when winning a Pontefract nursery (1m, soft) in October; interesting if conditions remain testing with a run under his belt.
runners.comment: Relished the conditions when winning a soft-ground Pontefract nursery in October

runners.name: Bemersyde
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability and, given his wide trip, he probably ran even better than it looked when fourth of ten on handicap debut at Chester (1m2f, good) 13 days ago (winner followed up in a 0-100 handicap at Epsom on Derby Day); respected.
runners.comment: Ran even better than it looked when fourth on handicap debut at Chester 13 days ago

runners.name: Qarreeb
runners.spotlight: Third in two Nottingham novices since returning in April, though the drop to 1m didn't appear to suit him last time; better expected back up in trip for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Drop to 1m didn't suit him last time; better expected back up in trip for handicap debut

races.race_name: Ladies Day At Sandown Park 3 July Handicap

runners.name: Lion Of Mali
runners.spotlight: Record in his first five handicaps read 21121, but finished over 7l behind Euphonia at Haydock (7f, soft) last month; may have found the ground too testing and now 10lb better off, but he still has work to do.
runners.comment: Over 7l behind Euphonia at Haydock last month; 10lb better off, but still has work to do

runners.name: Enamorus
runners.spotlight: Placed in all three starts over 6f/7f on turf last year, but proved a disappointing favourite on her reappearance/handicap debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) a fortnight ago; possible the run was needed after 258 days off, but she will need to step up considerably.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite on reappearance/handicap debut at Wolverhampton a fortnight ago

runners.name: Starryfield
runners.spotlight: Promising in his first three starts, but twice held since being gelded and was a particularly disappointing favourite in a Windsor maiden (6f, good to firm) last month; needs a resurgence.
runners.comment: Promising in his first three starts, but twice held since being gelded; needs a resurgence

runners.name: Southern Warrior
runners.spotlight: Made a successful handicap debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) for George Scott in March; held on turf/stable debut at Newmarket (6f, good) last month, but the step up to 7f may suit him; could sneak into the frame.
runners.comment: Held on turf/stable debut at Newmarket last month, but the step up to 7f may suit him

runners.name: Euphonia
runners.spotlight: Record on the AW reads 58856 but she took her record on turf to 411 with convincing wins at Leicester (7f, good) and Haydock (7f, soft); up another 10lb in her hat-trick bid, but the limit of her progress is still to be established.
runners.comment: Up 10lb in her hat-trick bid, but the limit of her progress is still to be established

runners.name: Lohoobb
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite for both handicaps, though he was rather stuck out on a limb when second of seven at Lingfield (7f, good) last time; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Probably ran better than it looked when second at Lingfield last time; shortlisted

runners.name: Galba
runners.spotlight: Best effort when third of seven in a Leicester novice (7f, good to firm) last month and didn't appear to stay on handicap debut at Windsor (1m, good to firm) a fortnight later; frame possibilities back over this trip.
runners.comment: Best effort when third in a Leicester novice last month; frame possibilities back over 7f

runners.name: Ecclefechan
runners.spotlight: Has twice come within a neck of winning, but his latest Windsor effort (6f, good to firm) not amongst his best and is now 0-11; first-time visor needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: Latest Windsor effort not amongst his best and is now 0-11; visor on

runners.name: Robert Anstruther
runners.spotlight: Has hinted at ability despite finishing unplaced in six starts; needs to find a bit more if he is to break his duck.
runners.comment: Unplaced in six starts; needs to find a bit more if he is to break his duck

runners.name: Kilkenny Warrior
runners.spotlight: Won his first two nurseries on the AW (6f/7f) last autumn, but the handicapper has been in charge since; still 6lb above her last winning mark and has a bit to prove back on turf with cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: Still 6lb above her last winning mark and has a bit to prove back on turf; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Dark Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Similar level of form in three starts over 7f on turf; needs to step up now switched to a handicap, but that is entirely possible.
runners.comment: Needs to step up now switched to a handicap, but that is entirely possible

runners.name: Kimbara
runners.spotlight: Best of three efforts in the spring when fourth of eight in a Newmarket maiden (7f, good) on penultimate outing; didn't stay 1m last time; could take a step forward now switched to a handicap.
runners.comment: Didn't stay 1m last time; could take a step forward now switched to a handicap

races.race_name: Billy Ocean At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap

runners.name: Wardlaw
runners.spotlight: Better effort switched to handicaps this year when second of five over 1m4f at Lingfield (AW) 11 days ago, worn down only towards the finish; won't need to pull out much more for new headgear (blinkers for cheekpieces) and a longer trip.
runners.comment: Improved second over 1m4f latest; won't need to find much more for new trip and headgear

runners.name: King Of Berkshire
runners.spotlight: Back on the up with a second all-the-way win of the year at Thirsk (1m4f, good) last Friday; has a likeable way of going about things and this longer trip could suit him even better; leading claims under a 6lb penalty.
runners.comment: Made all with ease over 1m4f at Thirsk last Friday and 6lb penalty may not stop him

runners.name: Red Rifle
runners.spotlight: Left his first three handicap efforts behind when making all with ease over 1m4f at Catterick (good to firm) 13 days ago, proving well suited by the significant step up in trip; an extra 2f ought to be within range on that evidence, although this is a much stiffer track.
runners.comment: Much improved for 1m4f when making all at Catterick latest; should stay further again

runners.name: Heroics
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice in novices and made a positive start to his handicap career when 7l fourth of 14 over 1m4f at York (good) four weeks ago; this is less competitive but didn't obviously shape at York as if he's crying out for this 2f longer trip.
runners.comment: Good fourth on 1m4f York handicap debut but didn't obviously shape as if needing further

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden

runners.name: Folsom Blues
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to two smart performers from same yard in Greek Mythology (RPR 106) and Atlantic Coast (RPR 104); shaped with plenty of promise when third on debut at the Curragh (6f, gd), running on encouragingly; bred to appreciate step up in trip and should improve plenty; one of three for the yard and strong contender.
runners.comment: Promising debut when third at the Curragh; bred to enjoy step up in trip; strong contender

runners.name: Premier View
runners.spotlight: Invincible Army colt cost 3,500euros as a yearling; dam 6f 2yo winner (RPR 88), half-sister to 1m4f-2m1f winner Mondain, out of unraced sister to 1m-1m2f Group 1 winner Manduro; makes debut for in-form yard; hood on first time.
runners.comment: Cheap yearling, dam 6f 2yo winner, half-sister to 1m4f-2m1f winner; hood on; in-form yard

runners.name: Lunar Empress
runners.spotlight: Zoustar filly; dam 7f winner (RPR 99), sister to Italian 1m2f Group 1 winner Laganore (dam of smart 1m4f/1m6f winner Real Dream), out of unraced half-sister to HK 1m2f/1m4f Group 1 winner Blazing Speed; strong pedigree; yard's 2yos usually come on for first outing but any market support should be noted.
runners.comment: Zoustar filly; dam 7f winner (RPR 99), sister to Italian 1m2f Group 1 winner; makes debut

runners.name: King's Fury
runners.spotlight: Kingman colt cost 160,000gns as a yearling; closely related to French 1m4f winner Senguun; dam unraced half-sister to French 1m2f winner Frankish, out of 1,000 Guineas winner Miss France, is granddaughter of 1m 2yo Group 1 winner/French Oaks second Miss Tahiti; looks second string out of three for the yard on debut; market can guide.
runners.comment: Kingman colt cost 160,000gns yearling; dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f winner; debut
runners.owner: Philip Antonacci & AC Crawford Farms LLC

runners.name: Wo Wo Never
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt; dam Group-placed 1m winner (RPR 107), closely related to US 8.5f winner Kalispera, half-sister to 7.4f 2yo winner La Dolce Vita, out of maiden close relative to 7f 2yo Group 1 winner Beethoven; well-bred type who looks third string on jockey bookings for yard; market can guide.
runners.comment: Wootton Bassett colt out of Group-placed 1m winner (RPR 107); one of three for yard; debut
runners.owner: Rectory Road Holdings Limited & Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Anthony Of Lisbon
runners.spotlight: Blackbeard colt cost 300,000gns as a yearling; half-brother to five winners inc Beckford (5f/6f inc 2yo Group 2/US; RPR 113), Lagenda (7f/1m inc 2yo; 91) and Poets Dance (5f inc 2yo; 88); dam 7f winner (89), half-sister to 1m2f/1m4f Group 1 winner Poet's Word; bred to be sharp, first-season sire; leading yard won last two renewals; leading chance of making a winning debut.
runners.comment: Blackbeard colt, 300,000gns yearling; half-brother to five winners inc very smart Beckford

runners.name: Golden Arizona
runners.spotlight: Showed speed but very green under pressure when 11th of 12 on debut at the Curragh over 6f 39 days ago; steps up in trip and needs to come on a lot to be in contention.
runners.comment: Very green when beating one home on debut; needs to come on plenty

runners.name: Give Me Liberty
runners.spotlight: Bred to be sharp, dam five-time 5f winner; made no impression when beaten a long way when last of seven at Naas 34 days ago; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: No impression made when beaten a long way on debut; hard to recommend

runners.name: Tina Tereshkova
runners.spotlight: Blew the start on debut at Listowel and never involved when last again at Fairyhouse; no reason to fancy.
runners.comment: Blew the start on debut at Listowel, never involved at Fairyhouse; no reason to fancy

runners.name: Chico Guapo
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to three winners including very useful Paddy's Day (5f/6f, inc 2yo); very green on debut when never in contention at Listowel 12 days ago; needs huge improvement to figure here up in trip.
runners.comment: Very green on debut when never in contention at Listowel 12 days ago; unlikely to threaten

races.race_name: Golf At Gowran Park Handicap

runners.name: Imnotleavinyou
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in three soft ground handicaps from 6f-1m this year before below-par run on quicker ground 14 days ago but bounced back on yielding at Limerick on Wednesday, beating Carvalhal by 1.25l; big chance if backing up quickly on soft.
runners.comment: Scored at Limerick two days ago; running consistently well on yielding to soft; big chance

runners.name: Fate's Gambit
runners.spotlight: Standout effort in five starts came on debut when beaten 0.5l at Dundalk (7f), well clear of the remainder; not progressed since but last three starts came in testing ground; likely to show more on handicap debut if the ground is dryer; wears first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Standout effort in five starts came on debut on AW; below form on soft since; h'cap debut
runners.owner: Gary Devlin & N W T Dalzell

runners.name: Gilts
runners.spotlight: Hard to glean much from three maiden runs, best effort coming when beaten 9l over 7f (gd) at Leopardstown in May; never involved at Dundalk 17 days ago; makes handicap debut.
runners.comment: Just mild promise in three maiden runs; makes h'cap debut off lowly mark; market can guide

runners.name: Moon Aura
runners.spotlight: Finished midfield in two 7f maidens before possibly not staying 9.5f at Ballinrobe 35 days ago; return to 7f will suit and ran well over this trip at Limerick when sixth behind the smart Signora; can figure on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Possibly didn't stay over 9.5f lto; drop back to 7f a positive; can figure on h'cap debut

runners.name: Sancerre Rock
runners.spotlight: Showed signs of promise when beaten 5.25l into sixth on debut at Limerick, making late inroads; bit below that level when never involved at the Curragh nine days ago; needs to be sharper early on from this draw; makes handicap debut for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Yard won this last year; promise on debut but slowly away last twice; makes h'cap debut

runners.name: Love Bomb
runners.spotlight: Down Royal handicap winner in September off 6lb higher; not fired since and two poor runs latest; step up in trip far from sure to suit.
runners.comment: Down Royal h'cap winner in Sept; two poor runs latest; step up in trip not sure to suit

runners.name: Sagasti
runners.spotlight: Improved from first two outings to finish third in 7f Dundalk maiden in December before being too free on stable and handicap debut when beaten 2.5l into sixth upped to a mile 91 days ago; shorter trip will suit and she looks on a workable mark.
runners.comment: Placed in 7f AW maiden before too free on h'cap debut over 1m; drop in trip will suit

runners.name: New Divide
runners.spotlight: Subject of each-way support on debut before well beaten; nothing to note on next two starts on soft ground; starts off handicapping off basement mark with tongue-strap on; 4lb wrong.
runners.comment: Nothing of note in three runs but did receive market support on debut; tongue-strap on

runners.name: Push Harder
runners.spotlight: Shaped well on handicap debut over 1m at Dundalk in November, backed that up when placed next time; returned from break when beaten 6.5l into sixth at same venue 50 days ago; capable off this mark but likely won't want ground to soften; 4lb wrong.
runners.comment: Running well since switched to AW h'caps; capable off mark but easing ground a question

runners.name: Pocket Jacks
runners.spotlight: Midfield in three maidens over 5f-7f, two coming on AW and on yielding at Naas over 5f; looked ready for 7f again when staying on to grab third on handicap debut at the Curragh nine days ago; should be more to come.
runners.comment: Looked ready for step up to 7f again when staying on late at the Curragh lto; more to come

runners.name: Apothic Red
runners.spotlight: Kept on encouragingly on handicap debut when sixth at the Curragh (6f, soft) and shaped similarly when fifth over same C&D lto; shapes as if extra trip will suit and each-way chance again.
runners.comment: Kept on well over 6f on two handicap starts; step up to 7f should suit; ew chance
runners.owner: Mrs Pauline McCourt/Donal O'Leary/Don't Ask The Wife Synd

runners.name: Cantilever
runners.spotlight: Has run to a similar level on all three starts over 6f on ground with a give; faded quickly when finishing midfield at the Curragh 54 days ago; gelded since and bred to appreciate step up in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Similar level in three starts; faded quickly lto; gelded and bred to enjoy extra trip
runners.owner: M R Sabatine & Mrs P Shanahan & C McLiney & S T Tsang

runners.name: Eschweiler
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in thee starts for the Moores last year, most notably when a 4l second to Starlight Time at Epsom (7f, gd) in July; gelded for new yard; off 336 days; drawn wide; market support would be interesting.
runners.comment: Showed some promise in three starts in Britain last year; gelded; new yard; off 336 days

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap

runners.name: Solana Beach
runners.spotlight: Promising debut second over C&D (7f, soft) when behind runner who ran at Group 1 level since; raced freely when fourth at Navan (1m, good) last month; intriguing handicap debutante for yard that had a winner at Navan on Sunday.
runners.comment: Two solid runs; intriguing handicap debutante for yard that had winner at Navan on Sunday

runners.name: Rion Rubette
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form with C&D win (soft) on latest when seeing off several of today's rivals (effort can be marked up as short of room then hampered over 1f out); up 3lb which is generous and should go well again.
runners.comment: Beat some of today's rivals over C&D last time and effort can be marked up; up 3lb

runners.name: Greydreambeliever
runners.spotlight: Some useful form in Britain for Karl Burke; good stable debut when runner-up in course handicap (1m, soft) and improved when filling the same spot behind Rion Rubette over C&D (soft) next time; faced a tall task in Listed grade at Naas on latest; front-runner is well drawn to attack.
runners.comment: Caught late on by Rion Rubette on penultimate; big player on that form; good draw
runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

runners.name: Luna Mia
runners.spotlight: Ran in some hot contests before getting off the mark in 6f Naas maiden (yielding to soft) in September; good second in Birdcatcher Nursery back there following month; needed the comeback run in Fairyhouse rated contest last week; trainer feels she's best on easy ground; live player.
runners.comment: Won her maiden on soft; needed the comeback run last week; respected

runners.name: Gloriously Glam
runners.spotlight: Solid fourth behind three of today's rivals over C&D in April; was gaining a fourth career victory when landing C&D claimer (good to yielding) last month; beaten favourite in Fairyhouse claimer on latest; fair mark returning to handicaps; respected.
runners.comment: Has some good course form including claimer win on penultimate; fair mark
runners.owner: Dooley Thoroughbreds & J Fairhurst & Deepak Dhand

runners.name: Una Matata
runners.spotlight: Excellent record on AW of late, winning four of her last 10 starts, three at this trip; big eyecatcher at Down Royal (7f, good) a fortnight ago when beaten 2l into fifth after a very slow start and not clear run 1f out but soft ground is a negative.
runners.comment: Big eyecatcher at Down Royal when everything went wrong but soft a negative here
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Mark McGuckin & Claire Wylie

runners.name: Greek Flower
runners.spotlight: On a long losing streak (since October 2023) but shaped well on several occasions, including on seasonal debut at Leopardstown; less encouraging run over C&D next time and folded tamely at Navan on Sunday; the jury is out.
runners.comment: Good season opener but not as good over C&D next time and folded tamely at Navan

runners.name: Fast Tara
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in handicaps this season but only one career win from 27 starts is a concern; finished last in Listed company at Navan on Sunday; new headgear combination is tried now.
runners.comment: Placed twice in handicaps this season but only one career win from 27 starts is a concern

runners.name: Pink Oxalis
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year, including over 1m at this track on soft in April; career-best when landing very competitive fillies' handicap at Leopardstown last September; stamina stretched here (9.5f) 11 days ago but beaten only 1l behind Rion Rubette when third over C&D on penultimate off current mark; contender.
runners.comment: Stamina stretched here lto but beaten only 1l over C&D on penultimate off current mark

races.race_name: Societies Welcome At Gowran Park Golf Club Handicap

runners.name: Hastily
runners.spotlight: Three-time turf winner, latest at Cork (6f, g-f) in July and finished a good second at Bellewstown in August over this trip; shown very little in two Flat starts and one over hurdles this year; hard to see winning.
runners.comment: Won at Cork (6f, g-f) last year; shown very little in last three starts; hard to fancy

runners.name: Limbocall
runners.spotlight: Showed hints of promise in two turf maidens, including over C&D on debut; disappointing on handicap debut at Leopardstown (1m1f, gd) when last seen; dropped 5lb and returns from 309 days off.
runners.comment: Disappointed on h'cap debut 309 days ago after hints of promise in maidens; dropped 5lb

runners.name: Queen Aethelflaed
runners.spotlight: Won off 2lb lower at Thurles (1m, gd) in October and placed at Leopardstown (7f, heavy) and on the AW over 10.5f; improved for first two runs this year to be beaten 2.5l behind Real Petite over C&D 11 days ago; 6lb better off with that rival; should build on that; one of two from yard.
runners.comment: Won off 2lb lower last year; returned to form lto when 2.5l behind Real Petite; claims

runners.name: Rock Etoile
runners.spotlight: Won twice and placed a further six times from 13 visits here; benefitted from sliding mark when beaten 2.75l into third over C&D 11 days ago; handles soft and well handicapped on best form; second over C&D off 11lb higher in October.
runners.comment: Course specialist benefitted from sliding mark when third over C&D lto; well treated
runners.owner: Conor McKenna & Patrick J McKenna & Emmet McKenna

runners.name: Mythical Rock
runners.spotlight: Gained breakthrough on AW over 10.5f in January; unlucky when sixth over C&D 23 days ago, short of room when travelling well; 2lb drop generous and handles soft; 1.25l behind Java Wood at Limerick on Wednesday; definite chance again.
runners.comment: Unlucky when sixth over C&D 23 days ago; second at Limerick on Wed; definite chance

runners.name: Simpson's Paradox
runners.spotlight: Last win came in September 2023 in heavy ground maiden at Killarney; ran well over C&D when sixth in October but below-par on last two runs; dropped 4lb.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D in Oct but below-par in two runs this year; dropped a further 4lb

runners.name: Miss Americana
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite at Fairyhouse (1m2f, gd) seven days ago, 1.5l behind Alto Sax; narrowly denied over C&D on soft in April off 2lb lower; drop back in trip no problem and should be winning one of these soon.
runners.comment: Beaten fav lto over 1m2f; narrow second over C&D in April; shorter trip fine; can go close

runners.name: Chipsrdown
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on positive handicap debut when well beaten at Killarney but bounced back in first-time visor when beaten 4l into second over C&D 23 days ago, albeit was well placed; each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Bounced back from poor effort in visor when second over C&D 23 days ago; ew claims

runners.name: Yorkshire Myth
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden in Britain but produced best effort when 2.25l second at Wolverhampton (9.5f) in October; tailed off on stable debut at Dundalk in January; blinkers go on, plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Tailed off on stable debut at Dundalk in Jan; blinkers on and plenty to prove

runners.name: Bright Image
runners.spotlight: Encouraging effort when fifth at the Curragh off 10lb higher in March 2024; returned from huge absence when 12th of 16 over C&D 23 days ago; will progress but likely not enough.
runners.comment: Returned from huge absence when 12th over C&D lto; will progress but needs plenty

runners.name: The Blue Panther
runners.spotlight: Fifth career win when revelling in the mud at Listowel (7f, heavy) in September off 3lb lower; hard to glean much positives from two runs this year and needs a big step forward.
runners.comment: Fifth win at Listowel on heavy in Sept; below form two starts this term; needs more
runners.owner: Ms Julianne McNally & Colette McNally & D McNally

runners.name: The Rock Factor
runners.spotlight: Finished midfield in a Naas bumper and three maidens from 1m to 10.5f, never landing a blow; hard to assess on handicap bow and market support would be interesting.
runners.comment: Midfield in a bumper and three maidens from 1m to 10.5f; market can guide

runners.name: Real Petite
runners.spotlight: Showed very little in 12 starts before improving out of nowhere, albeit appreciating the step back to this trip when defying 50-1 odds to land a C&D handicap 11 days ago, going 2.25l clear of Queen Aetheflaed; up 7lb and jury's out whether she can back it up.
runners.comment: Improved out of nowhere to land C&D h'cap 11 days ago decisively; up 7lb; needs to back up

runners.name: Morning Approach
runners.spotlight: 1-40 on turf; struggled on the AW throughout winter but showed more when fifth at Roscommon 32 days ago; has slid in the weights and chance if she can build on that.
runners.comment: Struggled on AW but showed more on turf lto; has slid in the weights; chance if building

runners.name: Roderick
runners.spotlight: First reserve; five-time winner on turf, latest at Naas (1m, y) off 1lb higher in July; two below-par efforts this year, including over C&D 64 days ago; declared at Leopardstown Thursday.
runners.comment: First reserve; latest win at Naas (1m, y) in July; two below-par efforts this year

runners.name: Selective Power
runners.spotlight: Three-time turf winner who made a positive return to turf at Bellwstown (1m, soft) in April before finishing ninth at Limerick on yielding; bolted up on soft at Roscommon off 1lb lower last year so can be in the mix if rain comes.
runners.comment: Three-time turf winner who fares best on soft; in decent form based on last two starts

races.race_name: Ladies Day July 25th Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Rayzera
runners.spotlight: Now 0-5 but was heading in the right direction when chasing home a very talented Ballydoyle odds-on favourite at Limerick (7f, yielding) in April; very disappointing at Dundalk on latest but return to a soft surface bodes well on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Disappointing at Dundalk on latest but return to soft surface bodes well on handicap debut
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Son Of Beauty
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner over 1m in March; beaten around 5l in Madrid Handicap (7f); stayed on late when beaten under 4l in 1m Naas premier handicap in late April; beaten 2l upped in trip at Leopardstown (1m1f, soft) eight days ago; fair chance from same mark.
runners.comment: Beaten 2l at Leopardstown (1m1f, soft) eight days ago; fair chance from same mark

runners.name: Rapide Vega
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden form, fourth to subsequent Chester Vase runner-up Proposition at Galway (7f, soft to heavy) in October and fifth in 1m course maiden (soft) on return that has worked out well; well beaten on handicap debut at Down Royal (good) but has been gelded since and should see improvement back on soft.
runners.comment: Some interesting maiden form in the mud; ground excuse for handicap debut; gelded

runners.name: Arctic Assassin
runners.spotlight: Left ordinary maiden form behind to win 1m Curragh handicap (soft) on seasonal return; weakened as if something amiss at Cork next time but vet reported gelding to be post race normal; bounce back required in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Won nicely at the Curragh on seasonal return; weakened as if something amiss at Cork

runners.name: Chapel Island
runners.spotlight: Juvenile form for Mick Mulvany wasn't progressive; well-backed for stable debut on 7f Fairyhouse return 15 days ago but never got involved; plenty to prove at present.
runners.comment: Well-backed for stable debut on 7f Fairyhouse return 15 days ago but never got involved

runners.name: Fallacy
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in trio of 1m maidens on the AW last year and at Cork on return; sent off 28-1 for handicap debut when fair third of seven at Navan (1m2f, yielding) and has something to build on now.
runners.comment: Sent off 28-1 for handicap debut when third at Navan and has something to build on

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Bofa Beach
runners.spotlight: Plenty of debut promise when fourth in good-class Curragh maiden last summer (good) but three months later never sighted in Goffs Million over same C&D on soft; back on track when third here (1m, good to yielding) on return but soft ground a concern.
runners.comment: Back on track when third here (1m, good to yielding) on return but soft ground a concern

runners.name: Sangreal
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly a 270,000gns yearling; promising debut run in 1m2f Leopardstown fillies' maiden (yielding) when chasing home a clear winner and 3l in front of Pink Coral; got going late when third at Cork (1m4f, soft to heavy) since; drop back in trip not ideal but holds Irish Oaks entry; respected.
runners.comment: Two solid maiden runs; drop back in trip not ideal but holds Irish Oaks entry; respected

runners.name: Miss Romagna
runners.spotlight: Built on Leopardstown debut sixth when runner-up to strong favourite at Cork (10.5f, soft), beaten 5l; the form of that maiden hasn't worked out but she's clearly got ability; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Second at Cork but form hasn't worked out; has ability so an each-way possibility

runners.name: Pink Coral
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly; finished behind a longer-priced stablemate (who was third) when fifth at Leopardstown on debut; cheekpieces applied (retained) when third of 10 at that venue (1m2f, good) on latest; improvement on the cards.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces applied (retained) when third of 10 at Leopardstown (1m2f, good) on latest
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Terra Di Siena
runners.spotlight: Yard saddled the last two winners of this race; 62,000euros yearling; sister to Listed-placed 1m winner Clemmie C (RPR 103), closely related to three winners; dam 1m/9.5f winner; second in Naas barrier trial last month; market noted.
runners.comment: Yard saddled the last two winners of this race; 62,000euros yearling; market noted

runners.name: Shambata
runners.spotlight: New Bay filly showed plenty of ability on Naas debut (10.5f, good) in April, slowly into stride but made late gains into fourth behind Glyfada who won again next time (runner-up also won since); bred to improve on this softer ground; leading player.
runners.comment: New Bay filly ran well on debut (form has been franked); bred to improve on soft ground
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Saga Gaga
runners.spotlight: Fair debut effort when midfield in Leopardstown maiden (1m, good) four weeks ago, keeping on late and beaten 7l; entitled to strip fitter now for in-form stable.
runners.comment: Fair debut effort when midfield in Leopardstown maiden; can run better now

runners.name: Zadawa
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam Listed-placed 9.4f/1m2f winner (RPR 108), half-sister to winners Zarir (Group-placed 1m4f/13.5f) and Zarafshan (2m), out of 12.5f Group 3 winning half-sister to outstanding 1m-1m4f Group 1 winner Zarkava; highly respected for top connections.
runners.comment: Dam out of 12.5f Group 3 winning half-sister to outstanding 1m-1m4f Group 1 winner Zarkava
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Lamar Valley
runners.spotlight: Price increased to 45,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to winners Imperial Mountain (7f AW inc 2yo; RPR 89) and Exhalation (1m AW; 72); dam 1m1f winner (stayed 1m4f; 81), market can guide.
runners.comment: Price increased to 45,000euros; dam 1m1f winner (stayed 1m4f; 81), market can guide

runners.name: Lady In The Gods
runners.spotlight: Time Test filly; 4,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Jonnie (11.5f; RPR 78) and Monna Vanna (French 1m/8.5f inc 2yo/AW); dam German 7f winner; best watched.
runners.comment: Time Test filly; 4,000gns yearling; half-sister to two winners; best watched

runners.name: Isabella Johnson
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in four of her seven bumpers but has her work cut out on Flat debut.
runners.comment: Made the frame in four of her seven bumpers but has her work cut out on Flat debut

runners.name: Vanir
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; encouragement to be taken from Curragh maiden effort (1m2f, good) last month when beaten 6l in midfield; wouldn't need to improve an awful lot to get involved here.
runners.comment: Beaten 6l in midfield on debut; wouldn't need to improve a lot to get involved here

runners.name: Tickle The Cat
runners.spotlight: Tailed off behind third-placed Sangreal on 1m4f Cork debut last month; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Tailed off behind third-placed Sangreal on 1m4f Cork debut last month; hard to fancy

runners.name: Spring Sprinter
runners.spotlight: From a family the trainer knows well; got outpaced on debut at this course (1m, yielding) 11 days ago; will need to take a sizeable step forward but the step up in trip is a plus.
runners.comment: Will need to take a sizeable step forward but the step up in trip is a plus

runners.name: Lady Luck
runners.spotlight: Very modest 41-rated filly who has struggled in handicaps of late; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Very modest 41-rated filly who has struggled in handicaps of late; cheekpieces

races.race_name: Packages At www.gowranpark.ie Race

runners.name: Trustyourinstinct
runners.spotlight: Won this race in 2023 and 2024; good form early last season, recording back-to-back wins in Listed/Group 3 contests at the Curragh over 1m4f/1m2f on good; hasn't hit top stride yet this season but is top-rated here; he would prefer decent ground but has form on soft.
runners.comment: Dual winner of this; hasn't hit top stride yet this season but is top-rated; prefers good

runners.name: Tiberius Thunder
runners.spotlight: Dundalk debut winner for Adrian Murray; showed improvement on second start for this yard when 6.5l behind Minnie Hauk in Curragh Group 2 (1m2f, good) last month; completely outclassed in Tattersalls Gold Cup at the Curragh 19 days ago; this is a lot easier but unproven on soft; blinkers.
runners.comment: Outclassed in Gold Cup at the Curragh 19 days ago; this is lot easier but unproven on soft
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Iron Fist
runners.spotlight: Two wins on soft ground last term; found 1m an inadequate test at the Curragh on seasonal debut; much better when second at Cork (10.5f, good to yielding) and third of 20 at the Curragh (1m2f, good) on latest; should give his running but has a big ask at the weights here.
runners.comment: In good form in handicaps; should give his running but has a big ask at the weights here

runners.name: Leopold The First
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; five of those runs have been on the AW and arrives here off the back of three runner-up finishes; however, rated just 77 and will struggle against much superior rivals.
runners.comment: In good form on AW but rated just 77 and will struggle against much superior rivals

runners.name: Terre De Vega
runners.spotlight: Won on good ground at two in France; ran only twice for William Haggas in 2024; same last year for this stable, the best of those a second at Leopardstown on the first of two late-season handicap outings; out of his depth in 10.5f Listed at Naas on return; plenty to find.
runners.comment: Out of his depth in Listed at Naas on return; plenty to find against a couple of these

races.race_name: Gowran Park Handicap

runners.name: Senna's Girl
runners.spotlight: 0-15 but placed five times; put two good runs back-to-back when fourth and third at Leopardstown and Limerick (both yielding); never got involved when last of 13 back in a handicap 26 days ago; has to bounce back but soft ground could be key to her; drawn wide.
runners.comment: Consistent run of form came to an end at Naas lto; handles soft but needs to bounce back

runners.name: Zipster
runners.spotlight: Dual winner on turf; 4.75l third over C&D, keeping on same pace in April; beaten favourite when ran a similar race to fill same spot at Leopardstown 58 days ago; dropped 2lb and below last winning mark, well-treated but needs things to fall right and a good pace to aim at.
runners.comment: Third in C&D h'cap in April; same spot at Leop 58 days ago; dangerous if pace to aim at

runners.name: Elusive Duke
runners.spotlight: Landed Leopardstown handicap (1m1f, gd) a year ago off 3lb lower and has been consistent since; returned when a 15l seventh eight days ago; needs to progress from that.
runners.comment: Winner over this trip at Leop last year; reasonable return eight days ago; outside chance

runners.name: Flying Fortress
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed soft ground last year, runner-up over 1m at Galway, went one better on heavy at Listowel off this mark; two runs this year not inspiring but soft ground combined with first-time blinkers could see a much-improved effort.
runners.comment: Heavy ground winner in Sept off this mark; below form this year; soft ground a plus

runners.name: Tero d'Rosa
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Ballinrobe (9.5f, gd) last month, showing good attitude to beat Majestic King by 0.75l; beaten 2.75l into sixth under penalty 31 days ago at Killarney from bad draw under more patient tactics; danger but soft ground an unknown.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Ballinrobe, not beaten far under penalty from bad draw lto; contender

runners.name: Ballymagreehan
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to land second win when making all at this track over 1m 23 days ago, stretching 4l clear of Chipsrdown; big 10lb hike for that and now faces longer trip and softer ground.
runners.comment: Landed second career win here 23 days ago by 4l over 1m; steps up in trip on softer ground

runners.name: Wrob And Wryan
runners.spotlight: Some good efforts on the AW last year, including a staying-on fifth when last seen 259 days ago; three turf runs have been poor however.
runners.comment: Consistent on AW and ran well over 10.5f lto but turf form a concern

runners.name: Faro Island
runners.spotlight: Landed 1m3f Down Royal handicap in September before disappointing favourite on the AW next time but had excuses; returned with encouraging fourth at Navan (1m2f, gd) 48 days ago, travelled well; set to come on for that and big player.
runners.comment: Won 1m3f h'cap last year, returned to form, fourth at Navan; more to come and big player

runners.name: Sir Jeremy
runners.spotlight: First reserve; improved from return and stable debut when landing Roscommon handicap (yielding) cosily 25 days ago; 5lb rise fine and handles all ground so definite contender if getting a run.
runners.comment: First reserve; improved from return to land Roscommon h'cap lto; 5lb more; chance if runs

runners.name: Doctor Grace
runners.spotlight: Notched third turf win at Roscommon (1m4f, gd) two starts ago, beating Makaiah by 2.25l; stumbled and unseated early lto at Fairyhouse; needs more from career-high mark.
runners.comment: Third turf win at Roscommon in May; stumbled and unseated lto; down in trip; handles soft

runners.name: Java Wood
runners.spotlight: Won handicap at Dundalk over 1m in November off 3lb lower; two solid runs on turf when fifth and fourth at the Curragh and Leopardstown; bounced back after below-par effort to score at Limerick (yielding) on Wednesday, keeping on well to beat Mythical Rock by 1.25l; leading claims if backing up quickly.
runners.comment: AW h'cap winner; scored on turf at Limerick on Wed; leading chance if backing up

runners.name: Starting Monday
runners.spotlight: Gained third win with C&D handicap success in April, beating Khormor Syd by 1.25l; ran respectably off 4lb higher last time at Cork; enjoys soft ground and needs more to defy mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner in April; ran okay off 4lb higher at Cork lto; needs more off new rating

runners.name: Bobby Kirova
runners.spotlight: Some promise in maidens last year but two poor efforts in handicaps this term; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Some promise in maiden runs but two poor efforts in h'caps this year; hard to fancy

runners.name: Booklight
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in maidens and ran a reasonable race when fourth at Cork (1m4f, soft) last year; backed that up when nearly making all at Leopardstown next time, beaten 0.25l into third; off 397 days and debuts for new trainer.
runners.comment: Nearly made all over 1m4f at Leop when last seen; off 397 days; stable debut

runners.name: Lauderdale King
runners.spotlight: Caught the eye with how he finished off over 1m2f at Cork on handicap debut 24 days ago, getting going late and running into third; can improve for that and is open to more improvement than most in this.
runners.comment: Fared better on h'cap debut at Cork lto, staying on strongly into third; big run likely

runners.name: Lady Arwen
runners.spotlight: Snapped lengthy losing run when landing 7.5f handicap at this track in April, beating Miss Americana by a short head; ran just okay off 4lb higher at Limerick since but dropped a further 2lb after; 3-7 here and enjoys soft ground so can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Snapped losing run at this track with h'cap win in April; below that at Lim; dropped 2lb

runners.name: Rio Doce
runners.spotlight: Set tough asks on first six starts between hurdles and the Flat, never landed a blow as a result; off 552 days and hard to assess on h'cap bow.
runners.comment: Set tough asks on first six starts and never featured; off 552 days ahead of h'cap bow

races.race_name: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Penny Benjamin
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; slowly into stride but eyecatching run when fast-finishing runner-up on Roscommon debut (7.5f, good to yielding) last month; dam 5f winner so drop in trip should be fine and she's a massive player even though Colin Keane is on her stablemate.
runners.comment: Finished well to take second at Roscommon after slow break; drop in trip fine; big player
runners.owner: T Howley Jnr & Eoin Banville & Peter Robin Parkhill

runners.name: Switching Sides
runners.spotlight: Kodiac colt bought back for 52,000euros as a yearling; excellent debut effort when runner-up to Ballydoyle-trained hotpot at the Curragh last month (6f, good); sets a good standard.
runners.comment: Runner-up to Ballydoyle-trained hotpot at the Curragh last month; sets a good standard

runners.name: Bit Of A Buzz
runners.spotlight: Sent off 18-1 on Curragh debut (5f, good) and ran a decent race to finish 5.75l third; ran to a similar level when fourth at Cork (5f, soft) next time but the form doesn't look that strong; jockey/trainer combination won two of last three runnings; up in trip and well drawn.
runners.comment: Has run to a decent level without threatening; combination won two of last three runnings

runners.name: Razmataz
runners.spotlight: Starspangledbanner colt cost 5,000gns; brother to Italian 7f/1m1f winner Sentiero; dam placed 1m2f, sister to 6f-9.5f winner Defendant, out of unraced sister to champion sprinter Danehill; third in 7f Naas barrier trial last month; market can guide.
runners.comment: Starspangledbanner colt cost 5,000gns; third in 7f Naas barrier trial last month

runners.name: Nando Royale
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; resold 8,000euros yearling; dam unraced half-sister to winners Yangtze (1m6f/1m7f) and Evangelist (7.5f-11.6f), out of Park Hill Stakes winning close relation to smart 1m4f winner Warringah; market can guide.
runners.comment: Second runner for yard; resold 8,000euros yearling; market can guide
runners.owner: Kiva Cromwell & Coragh Bloodstock Limited & Fergus Grimes

runners.name: Alrashdeyah
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; Churchill filly was sent off 11-2 for Cork debut when 4.25l fourth of nine (6f, soft) last month; entitled to strip fitter now and is respected for top yard.
runners.comment: Sent off 11-2 for Cork debut when 4.25l fourth of nine (6f, soft); should improve

runners.name: Trean
runners.spotlight: Bayside Boy colt; resold 20,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners Dark Lucinda (9.4f; RPR 85) and Wondrous Ways (5f 2yo; 54); dam 1m2f winner, half-sister to French 1m2f Listed winner; stable 26% with 2yos last five years; hooded; player.
runners.comment: Bayside Boy colt; resold 20,000euros yearling; stable 26% with 2yos last five years; hood

runners.name: Banks Of The Boyne
runners.spotlight: Third foal cost 3,000euros; half-brother to Danish 7.5f 2yo winner Supreme Shine; dam unplaced 7f-1m4f (RPR 60), sister to 7f 2yo winner, half-sister to several winners; doesn't have a standout profile but yard 3-11 with 2yos this season; gelded.
runners.comment: Cost 3,000euros; doesn't have standout profile but yard 3-11 with 2yos this season; gelded

runners.name: Magna Fortuna
runners.spotlight: Magna Grecia filly cost 9,000euros; half-sister to winners Stage Magic (7f/1m 2yo inc Group 3; RPR 108) and Bystander (1m/1m2f AW; 96); dam 7.4f 2yo winner, half-sister to 1m4f Grade 1 winner Talismanic, out of US 1m Grade 3 winner; yard 0-14 with 2yos last five seasons.
runners.comment: Magna Grecia filly cost 9,000euros; nice profile but yard 0-14 with 2yos last 5 seasons

runners.name: Just One Show
runners.spotlight: Showcasing colt; 88,000euros foal, 30,000euros yearling; dam unraced half-sister to 6f/7f Group 3 winner Kiyoshi and 2m-2m3f hurdle/chase winner Red Riverman; stable can ready one; market noted.
runners.comment: Showcasing colt; 88,000euros foal, 30,000euros yearling; yard can ready one

runners.name: Brazilian Diva
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in third on 5f Dundalk debut; tailed off when outclassed in 6f Curragh Group 3 last month; this is much easier but she will need to find improvement.
runners.comment: Well beaten in third on 5f Dundalk debut; tailed off when outclassed in 6f Curragh Group 3

runners.name: Lucy's Promise
runners.spotlight: Third runner for yard; 2,000euros yearling; dam unplaced 1m/8.5f (RPR 68), out of maiden half-sister to 5f 2yo Group 2 winner Sand Vixen; finished one place behind Ivory River in 7f Naas barrier trial last month.
runners.comment: E2,000 yearling finished one place behind Ivory River in 7f Naas barrier trial last month

runners.name: Glacial Force
runners.spotlight: Showcasing filly; £40,000 yearling; dam Italian 7f 2yo winner, half-sister to useful Australian 7f-1m3f winner Seat Of Power, out of US 8.5f winning half-sister to French 2,000 Guineas winner Falco; yard has modest strike-rate with 2yos.
runners.comment: Showcasing filly; £40,000 yearling; dam Italian 7f 2yo winner; yard modest with 2yos

runners.name: Oristown Boy
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; 7,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners Spanish Blaze (6f/7f inc 2yo; RPR 95), Octophobia (7f 2yo; 73) and Billy Byrnes (Danish 6f 2yo/1m1f); dam French 1m winner; yard 1-34 with 2yos; tough draw.
runners.comment: 7,000euros yearling; half-brother to several winners; yard 1-34 with 2yos; tough draw
runners.owner: Ms Lisa Sheridan & Patrick M O'Brien & Larry Murray

runners.name: Ivory River
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; 30,000euros yearling; half-sister to 7f AW winner Colombidea (RPR 86); dam French Listed-placed 5.5f 2yo winner (94); didn't show much when seventh in 7f Naas barrier trial last month.
runners.comment: 30,000euros yearling didn't show much when seventh in 7f Naas barrier trial last month

runners.name: Elyatta
runners.spotlight: Elzaam colt; 18,000euros yearling; brother to Spanish 6f 2yo winner Mi Nino; dam unplaced half-sister to winners Lord Campari (useful 1m) and Charleston Belle (6f), out of 6f Listed winner; booking of Colin Keane a plus; wide draw a negative.
runners.comment: Elzaam colt; 18,000euros yearling; booking of Colin Keane a plus; wide draw a negative

runners.name: Stay Curious
runners.spotlight: Dwelt at the start when never a factor on 7.5f Roscommon debut four weeks ago; others preferred; gelded.
runners.comment: Dwelt at the start when never a factor on 7.5f Roscommon debut four weeks ago; gelded

runners.name: The Drambling Man
runners.spotlight: Reserve; hung right when beaten 8.5l on 5f Cork debut; well beaten at Listowel (6f); safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Hung right when beaten 8.5l on 5f Cork debut; well beaten at Listowel (6f); reserve

runners.name: Portaliffe Phil
runners.spotlight: Beat just one rival home when given a forceful ride on 6f debut at Cork last month; unlikely winner.
runners.comment: Beat just 1 rival home when given a forceful ride on 6f debut at Cork last month; unlikely

races.race_name: Winter Festival Early Bird Handicap

runners.name: Nakamura
runners.spotlight: Was busy at two, winning nurseries at Navan and Cork over this trip; below his best this term but not bad runs on two latest; down 3lb but needs more; looks stable second-string.
runners.comment: Not bad runs on two latest; down 3lb but needs more; looks stable second-string

runners.name: Joyful Tidings
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f Tipperary maiden on debut last year, beating a subsequent Listed winner; didn't really go on from that after a break in 2025; best run this term came over 5f at Cork last time; needs to show he is as good over this trip.
runners.comment: Best run this term came over 5f at Cork last time; has to show is as good over this trip

runners.name: Midnight Dusk
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Dundalk maiden in December and solid runs next three starts between 5f and 6f; his poor showing at Navan last time might have been due to the week turnaround from Naas; can go well if rebounding.
runners.comment: Below-par run last time might have come too soon; can go well if rebounding

runners.name: Sovereign Cry
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f Dundalk maiden last winter but was too keen when poor on return at Naas over 6f; two better efforts over 5f since but the suspicion is that he is better over that trip.
runners.comment: Decent efforts over 5f last twice but the suspicion is that he is better over that trip
runners.owner: John B O'Hagan & Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Coincidental Glory
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk nursery last winter and some fine efforts in defeat since; however, is off the same mark as when beaten 5l at the Curragh last time so needs more.
runners.comment: Off the same mark as when beaten 5l at the Curragh last time so needs more

runners.name: Stone Bear
runners.spotlight: Won for the third time when a ready enough winner over C&D two weeks ago; a 7lb rise is fair enough and a decent claimer taking off 7lb helps offset that rise; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Won over C&D two weeks ago; a 7lb rise is fair; claimer takes off 7lb; on the shortlist

runners.name: Nazario
runners.spotlight: Ran several solid races in defeat last year for Natalia Lupini up to a mile; gelded since and looks to be new yard's selected on jockey bookings so can run well if ready enough.
runners.comment: Looks the stable selected on jockey bookings so can run well if ready enough

runners.name: Venetian Star
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run several fine races in defeat for Johnny Murtagh and this yard; raced a bit keenly over 7f here last time when just denied and should cope with the drop in trip; considered.
runners.comment: Raced a bit keenly over 7f here last time when just denied; should cope with drop in trip

runners.name: Absoluteassignment
runners.spotlight: Showed hints of ability in three maidens at 7f; did better when dropped to this C&D on handicap debut when 1.25l behind Stone Bear; 5lb better off with that rival and tongue-tie worn then is retained; a player.
runners.comment: Was 1.25l behind Stone Bear over C&D on handicap debut; 5lb better off today; a player

runners.name: All The Girls
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk maiden in good style last October but hasn't really gone on from that, albeit some solid efforts; found 5f too sharp back at Dundalk last time and this trip will suit better; considered down 4lb.
runners.comment: Found 5f too sharp at Dundalk last time; this trip will suit better; considered down 4lb
runners.owner: Elaine Shevlin & Cormac Farrell & Ms Denise McCourt

runners.name: Unidos
runners.spotlight: His mark of 72 is based on his decent debut effort at Naas last year but he has been well below that level twice since; tongue-tie on for handicap debut and is likely capable of better.
runners.comment: Promise on debut but poor twice since; handicap debut and is likely capable of better

runners.name: Breaking Dawn
runners.spotlight: Two decent runs in defeat as a 2yo; won a heavy-ground 6f maiden at the Curragh on return but disappointed in the Madrid handicap over 7f a week later; freshened up since and there should be enough ease in the ground so can be a player.
runners.comment: Won a 6f maiden but disappointed over 7f a week later; freshened up since and a player

runners.name: Dancing Saxon
runners.spotlight: Some solid runs in defeat as a 2yo, including over C&D (hung left); got off the mark in a 7f Dundalk conditions race in February with cheekpieces on; headgear left off on return and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Won a 7f Dundalk conditions race in February with cheekpieces; headgear left off on return

races.race_name: Maher Tractor Sales Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Jampa Ling
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner at Dundalk over this trip; was poor over C&D two starts ago in first-time blinkers, but much better back here last week with no headgear; poor draw but a player.
runners.comment: Was better back here last week with no headgear; poor draw but a player

runners.name: Rosato
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner has run well in defeat at this track; well beaten both starts this term, but is now 4lb below last winning mark so isn't dismissed.
runners.comment: Well beaten both starts this term but is now 4lb below last winning mark; not dismissed

runners.name: Son Of Wind
runners.spotlight: A 5f AW winner when trained in UK; well beaten in four runs for this yard and has to do much better.
runners.comment: Well beaten in four runs for this yard and has to do much better

runners.name: Mini Cotai
runners.spotlight: Maiden hasn't been beaten far at times, but lesser efforts last two runs including over C&D last time; 3lb out of the handicap; hood added.
runners.comment: Lesser efforts last two runs incl' over C&D last time; 3lb out of the handicap; hood added

runners.name: Hero Of The Hour
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner for his former yard between 5f-6f on turf plus AW; some fair AW runs in spring; 2lb below last winning mark, so not ruled out.
runners.comment: Seven-time winner for his former yard; 2lb below last winning mark so not ruled out

runners.name: Dervo Annie
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form on second handicap start at Down Royal last time over 7f when just denied; raised 5lb for that but has a solid chance if as effective over this trip; Keane stays aboard too.
runners.comment: Just denied over 7f last time; up 5lb but a solid chance if as effective over this trip

runners.name: Luminessa
runners.spotlight: Mild promise in maidens; beaten about 7l in two handicaps including over C&D last time (wide draw) so has to do more; dropped 3lb a help.
runners.comment: Beaten about 7l in two handicaps including over C&D last time so has to do more
runners.owner: Scott Sterling & Darragh Cuffe & Conor Keohane

runners.name: Sayonara
runners.spotlight: Some creditable runs in defeat at Dundalk over the winter at 5f-6f; gelded since his last run; inexperienced rider aboard for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Handicap bow; gelded since his last run; inexperienced rider aboard

runners.name: Whatswrongnow
runners.spotlight: A maiden after 27 starts but went close over C&D two runs back; well beaten back here since with cheekpieces added; has to rebound with the headgear removed.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D two runs back; well beaten back here since when wore cheekpieces

runners.name: Another Nice'day
runners.spotlight: Best run came over 5f at Down Royal last year in cheekpieces; well beaten over 7f there on return and has to improve plenty.
runners.comment: Well beaten over 7f at Down Royal on return and has to improve plenty

runners.name: The Love Machine
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-16; well beaten in five starts for this yard in 2026 and isn't easy to fancy on that evidence.
runners.comment: Well beaten in five starts for this yard in 2026 and isn't easy to fancy on that evidence

runners.name: Spiced Gold
runners.spotlight: Went closest to winning when beaten 3.75l at Down Royal last time over 5f; this trip should suit better and is well drawn, so of interest.
runners.comment: Outpaced over 5f last time; this trip should suit better and is well drawn so of interest

runners.name: El Fontenaro
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 17 starts; a decent run at Sligo on return but was below that level over C&D since, though didn't get the clearest of passages.
runners.comment: Maiden was below form over C&D last time, though didn't get the clearest of passages

runners.name: I'm Spartacus
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner scored for the first time on turf when winning over C&D last week; raised 9lb for that but a decent claimer takes off 7lb so that helps; not the best draw but still a big player.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last week; up 9lb but a decent claimer takes off 7lb; not the best draw

runners.name: Dandy Land
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in three maidens but ran with promise on seasonal/handicap debut at the Curragh recently; with improvement from that she should go closer today.
runners.comment: Promise on seasonal/handicap debut at the Curragh recently; should go closer today

runners.name: Step Back In Time
runners.spotlight: All four of mare's wins have come at Navan between 5f-6f but has run well in defeat at this track; way below form on her last three starts, but is back to last winning mark so not dismissed.
runners.comment: Way below form on her last three starts but is back to last winning mark so not dismissed

races.race_name: Maher Tractor Sales Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Jackandthefox
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 21 runs but placed a number of times, including in this race last year; can improve from seasonal debut over C&D so could be involved.
runners.comment: Maiden after 21 runs but can improve from seasonal debut over C&D

runners.name: Hooves Your Daddy
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run well in 5f handicaps the last twice; flew home at Down Royal last time and looks to have a big chance over this trip which looks like it will suit.
runners.comment: Flew home over 5f at Down Royal last time and looks to have a big chance over this trip

runners.name: One Cool Bear
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been poor over 7f last twice, including here last time; has to get back to his better 6f runs at Dundalk and Naas.
runners.comment: Maiden has been poor over 7f last twice and has to get back to his better 6f runs

runners.name: Rodeeve
runners.spotlight: Maiden posted her best previous runs over 7f but should have finished closer over C&D last time when not getting a clear run; down 3lb; player.
runners.comment: Maiden should have finished closer over C&D last time; down 3lb; player

runners.name: Dynamic Force
runners.spotlight: Four wins up to a mile and even ran well over hurdles last year; was keen over 7f last time at Down Royal and can run well again back to 6f.
runners.comment: Four wins up to a mile; was keen over 7f last time and can run well again back to 6f

runners.name: Alnofoor
runners.spotlight: Came back from over four years off last year; hit form this term, winning at Navan (6f) and Bellewstown (5f) before a fine 3rd at Curragh; those runs were on soft but has handled good ground so should be involved.
runners.comment: Won at Navan and Bellewstown this year and a fine 3rd at Curragh; should be involved

runners.name: Ukiyo
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f at Cork off 5lb lower last year for Gavin Cromwell and some good runs in defeat since; hit and miss on last four starts but a player if on a going day.
runners.comment: Won at Cork off 5lb lower last year and some good runs since, though not last time

runners.name: Due In June
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in three maidens over 7f but can do better in this grade; watch the market for clues.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in three maidens but can do better in this grade; watch the market

runners.name: Andosky
runners.spotlight: Second on handicap debut last year at Sligo but not as good since; her regression continued with a poor run over C&D last time; not easy to fancy.
runners.comment: Her regression continued with a poor run over C&D last time; not easy to fancy

runners.name: Glen Breeze
runners.spotlight: Maiden doesn't look completely straightforward; poor in blinkers two starts ago, then hung left when second over C&D last week; can be involved with a repeat; tongue-tie on.
runners.comment: Hung left when second over C&D last week; be involved with a repeat; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Prime Sign
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f at the Curragh last year off a 1lb higher mark; some decent runs after but was poor there on return in first-time cheekpieces; can do better now.
runners.comment: Won over 6f at Curragh last year; poor there on return but can do better now

runners.name: Reponse Finale
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 20 starts but some good runs; similar efforts in defeat over C&D the last twice, but needs a bit more to make the breakthrough.
runners.comment: Similar efforts in defeat over C&D the last twice but needs a bit more

runners.name: Bel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Maiden was beaten about 8l on last two runs at Sligo and Navan so needs to find more off this mark.
runners.comment: Beaten about 8l on last two runs so needs to find more off this mark
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: Monastere
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three handicap starts from 6f-8f and has find significant improvement.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three handicap starts and has to find significant improvement

runners.name: Livingston Range
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner has run well in defeat at this venue too; ran a fine race at Navan on seasonal debut and should be involved with improvement.
runners.comment: Ran a fine race at Navan on seasonal debut and should be involved with improvement

runners.name: Dubai Opulence
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in three maidens over 6f-7f but can do better in this grade; watch the market for clues.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in three maidens but can do better in this grade; watch the market
runners.owner: Syndicates Racing/W Jones/N O'Hare/Kabin Racing Syndicate

runners.name: Verhoyen
runners.spotlight: Veteran is an eight-time winner, with the last of those at this track in 2024; ran a fine race over C&D from a wide draw lately and can be involved again.
runners.comment: Ran a fine race over C&D from a wide draw lately and can be involved again
runners.owner: Paul Cullen & Joseph E Keeling & M C Grassick

races.race_name: Pat's Carpets Race

runners.name: Zodiac Bear
runners.spotlight: Much-improved on final two runs of 2025, both at Leopardstown over this trip; won a maiden in fine style before finishing third in a Group 3; handles varying ground conditions and stayed on to finish fourth in a Group race at Leopardstown Thursday; chance in this lower grade if turning out quickly
runners.comment: Fourth in a Group 3 at Lepardstown Thursday night; can be a big player running again

runners.name: Cowardofthecounty
runners.spotlight: Group 3-winning 2yo over this trip but didn't fire in three runs last year; the best of two runs this term came at Gowran last time when ahead of Tina's Indian; the filly that won that event, City Of Memphis, has since won twice at Group level; a big player.
runners.comment: Second at Gowran last time to a filly that has since won twice at Group level; big player

runners.name: Frescobaldi
runners.spotlight: Useful 2yo for Aidan O'Brien last year, winning a 7f York maiden and running well in two Listed races; solid effort on debut for this yard in Navan Listed race but well below that at Newbury since; has to rebound now gelded.
runners.comment: Solid effort in Navan Listed race but well below that at Newbury since; now gelded
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell & R P Behan & Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Ocean's Breath
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Dundalk maiden in April then had excuses for a lesser run in a Naas handicap; bounced back to win a C&D conditions race a week ago in a first-time tongue-tie; that is retained but has to improve in this stronger contest.
runners.comment: Won a C&D conditions race a week ago but has to improve in this stronger contest

runners.name: Love Billy Boy
runners.spotlight: Four wins up to 7.5f and in varying ground conditions; was beaten a long way on return over 6f at the Curragh in March; can do a lot better today but others preferred.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on return over 6f at the Curragh in March; can do a lot better today

runners.name: Tina's Indian
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been at the Curragh over 6f-7f and in varying ground; solid efforts all three starts this year including over 6f at this track last week; the cheekpieces worn then are replaced by a tongue-tie; needs more on these terms.
runners.comment: Solid efforts all three starts this year including over 6f here last week; needs more
runners.owner: C F Harrington & James John Hughes

runners.name: Tolebi
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; winner over 7f for Andre Fabre in France and twice placed in Listed grade; however, then lost his form badly and was poor on stable debut in March; needs a revival.
runners.comment: Lost his form badly in France and was poor on stable debut in March; needs a revival

runners.name: Moss Tucker
runners.spotlight: Former winner of the Group 1 Flying Five but isn't at that level anymore; two respectable runs behind Mission Central at Naas over 5f this term; needs to do better but any easing of the ground will help.
runners.comment: Two fair runs at Naas over 5f this term; needs to do better; easing ground will help

runners.name: Queen Of The Bogs
runners.spotlight: Was a comfortable winner of a 1m1f Curragh maiden on second start but couldn't get competitive in a Killarney Listed race when last seen; needs to do more down in trip on return.
runners.comment: Held in a Killarney Listed race when last seen; needs to do more down in trip on return

races.race_name: Dino Day At Fairyhouse 8th July Handicap

runners.name: Coulstys Way
runners.spotlight: First reserve; in good form last summer, winning off 12lb lower at Roscommon (1m4f, good) and placing multiple times; below her best on heavy at Listowel and on hurdling debut at Navan when last seen in November; not ruled out back on a sound surface.
runners.comment: Reserve; in good form last summer and not ruled out on seasonal return

runners.name: Boxing Great
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the ninth attempt in a 2m handicap at Dundalk in December (2m); creditable eighth in a higher quality Chester handicap over an extended 2m2f last time (good); in the mix.
runners.comment: AW winner in Dec' and creditable eighth in a Chester h'cap latest; in the mix

runners.name: Night Moon
runners.spotlight: Impressive Listowel winner in final start for David Marnane off 60 (1m4f, soft to heavy), however, that came almost three years ago; failed to make an impact in opening three maiden hurdles for new yard, but his distant second of eight at Down Royal when last seen was more encouraging; others preferred.
runners.comment: Impressive winner when last seen on Flat in 2023; 4 hurdle runs since a bit disappointing

runners.name: Just Keep Flying
