runners.spotlight: Won a Goodwood maiden (1m2f, good to firm) on debut for the Gosdens; creditable efforts on turf and AW following that; purchased for 20,000gns in December; decent sixth of 16 at Gowran (1m4f, yielding) on seasonal return when behind the stable first string; Foley rides now in a first-time hood trying this trip for the first time.
runners.comment: Debut m'den winner for Gosdens; decent stable debut and Foley now rides

runners.name: Voice Of Reason
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose sort is 1-10 on Flat turf; won on the AW at Dundalk (1m4f) off 3lb lower in January and ran well at that venue following that; well beaten back on turf at Gowran 11 days ago (1m4f, yielding); suspicion he's not as effective on this surface.
runners.comment: Won on AW in Jan' but 1-10 on turf and well beaten on this surface last time

runners.name: Maeve Brennan
runners.spotlight: Good efforts in three Curragh maidens last season on softer conditions; decent fifth on handicap debut at the Curragh on seasonal return (1m4f, soft to heavy); improved for good to yielding ground over 1m2f at Navan next time when second of 23 behind an easy winner; disappointed up to this trip at the Curragh last time.
runners.comment: Good efforts in opening two h'caps; poor over this trip last time
runners.owner: Eleanora Kennedy & Mrs J S Bolger

runners.name: Jr Mahon
runners.spotlight: Some promising runs in defeat last season; okay run over hurdles at Bellewstown in April but his tailed off finish at Sligo last month is concerning.
runners.comment: Some promise in defeat last season but tailed off finish over hurdles latest is concerning

runners.name: Carthusian
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in five maidens last term; faded into seventh on handicap bow/seasonal return at Ballinrobe (1m5f, good to yielding); finished in midfield of 24-runner handicap at the Curragh last month (1m6f, good); not ruled out.
runners.comment: Showed ability in five m'dens; midfield in second h'cap start latest; not ruled out

runners.name: Alba Chiara
runners.spotlight: All her best form has come at Down Royal, notably winning the 2m2f Ulster Cesarewitch last September off 63; never sighted on her Curragh comeback last month (1m6f, good); good ground suits and may improve over that first run back but must prove she can give her best away from her favoured northern track.
runners.comment: Best form at Down Royal inc' 2m2f win last term off 63; never sighted on Curragh return
runners.owner: Mrs Tracey O'Hare & D W Sloan & William Jones

runners.name: Earthwatch
runners.spotlight: Sandown 1m handicap winner for William Haggas (1m, good) off 70; poor in a claimer and two maiden hurdles for new yard; carried out early at Clonmel last time; the cheekpieces are new.
runners.comment: British 1m h'cap winner off 70; poor form for new yard inc' over hurdles

runners.name: Paradise Lost
runners.spotlight: 1-15 on Flat with the win coming at Roscommon in 2024 (1m4f, soft) off 64; decent efforts over hurdles this year and shaped with promise back on Flat at Gowran last time when fifth (1m6f, good to yielding); will appreciate any rain.
runners.comment: Decent efforts over hurdles this year and promising return to Flat latest; suited by soft

runners.name: Guy's Girl
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; promise in maidens last year at 1m-1m1f; second handicap run over 1m2f here last time (good) was just okay.
runners.comment: Reserve; promise in m'dens; h'cap run here last time was just okay

runners.name: High Honour
runners.spotlight: Sole Flat win came in 2023 although has mostly been competing over hurdles in recent times; improved markedly over poor Navan seasonal return when fourth at Roscommon (1m4f, good), staying on after slowly away; below that at the Curragh last time; on career low mark.
runners.comment: Sole Flat win came in 2023; decent fourth at Roscommon but below that level latest

runners.name: Bruant
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; dual French Flat winner in 2024 at 1m1f/1m3f; both hurdling wins have come at Downpatrick, including when successful over 2m3f last time (good to yielding); tongue tie on but Flat runs this term not so encouraging.
runners.comment: Reserve; Flat runs this term not encouraging but successful over hurdles last time

runners.name: Not Just Any Eagle
runners.spotlight: 4-30 on turf, along with one AW victory; last success came off 2lb lower at Leopardstown last year (1m4f, good); struggled on soft ground at Gowran on seasonal return, but improved on that when seventh of 16 back there last time (1m4f, yielding), a nose behind Just Keep Flying; heading in the right direction.
runners.comment: Added fourth turf win last year off 2lb lower; improved over seasonal return last time

runners.name: Slurricane
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler who has won twice on the Flat, including off 4lb lower at Galway in October (1m4f, heavy); ran respectably over this trip at Gowran in April (1m6f, soft); would appreciate soft conditions.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner ran respectably over this trip when last seen; best on soft

runners.name: Zoffman
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner in 2022 and added a maiden hurdle last year; just okay Flat handicap runs this season before tailed off over hurdles at Ballinrobe last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Just okay Flat h'cap runs this season and tailed off over hurdles last time

runners.name: Harry's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner in 2022; good effort at Gowran (1m6f, soft) when third and backed that up at Killarney when second of nine over 2m1f on good ground; player given those recent efforts.
runners.comment: Placed last twice over 1m6f/2m1f on different ground recently; player given those efforts

runners.name: Dutch Gold
runners.spotlight: A long time since his last win, under Keane at the Curragh in 2023 off 13lb higher (1m2f, soft to heavy); not far away again under Keane last summer at Cork (1m4f, good); form since then has been poor, but interesting that Keane is aboard again now.
runners.comment: Last win came in 2023 under Keane; poor recent form but interesting that he's up again
runners.owner: Albert Dravins & Exors of the Late Eamonn Scanlon

runners.name: Sanctity Of Space
runners.spotlight: Good efforts in final two maidens for Joseph O'Brien; 3.25l runner-up at Thurles over extended 2m (good) in first-time cheekpieces; took fourth of 11 at Navan next time (1m6f, yielding); makes handicap/stable debut now.
runners.comment: Goof efforts in final two m'dens for Joseph O'Brien; makes h'cap/stable debut

runners.name: Granville Street
runners.spotlight: Almost added to his sole Flat win at Navan when beaten a neck in a three-way photo (1m5f, good to yielding); favourite off this new mark at Ballinrobe but could only manage fifth (1m5f, good to yielding); in the mix.
runners.comment: Almost added second Flat win when beaten a neck at Navan; only fifth next time; chance

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden

runners.name: Now Maybe
runners.spotlight: Sets a decent standard here off a mark of 85; beaten a nose over 7f at Dundalk on second handicap start in November and made a positive seasonal return at Navan in April when a 3l third of 18 (1m2f, good to yielding); Navan handicap debut last time was below his best (1m2f, yielding); evidence to date suggests this new trip is within reach.
runners.comment: Sets a decent standard following positive m'den runs; new trip should suit

runners.name: Mr Ballynonty
runners.spotlight: Chased home two experienced rivals with decent form on debut at Navan (1m2f, yielding); that was a positive first run and marks him out as a big player with normal improvement.
runners.comment: Chased home two experienced rivals on debut; that marks him out as a big player

runners.name: Dreamkeeper
runners.spotlight: Solid sixth at Navan over 1m2f (good to yielding) at 80-1; didn't appear to get home on soft ground at Cork over an extended 1m2f last time; up in trip now with Keane aboard for the first time.
runners.comment: Solid sixth at Navan on debut; faded into fifth last time; Keane rides for the first time
runners.owner: Mrs H Shanahan & Mrs Jane Shanahan

runners.name: Tanzanite Diamond
runners.spotlight: Similar efforts in opening maidens but improved to take third over 1m2f here last time when third despite 125-1 odds (good); others preferred.
runners.comment: Improved to take third over 1m2f here in third m'den despite 125-1 odds; others preferred

runners.name: Ma Belle Etoile
runners.spotlight: Bumper/hurdles/chase winner makes her Flat debut now; has run well after breaks before and won on good ground; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Bumper/hurdles/chase winner makes her Flat debut now; interesting contender
runners.owner: P Haughey & P M Haughey & John Ball

runners.name: Notahairoutofplace
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 5f/6f winner Dc Flyer (inc 2yo/AW; RPR 69); dam unraced half-sister to useful winners Eastern Romance (5f 2yo inc Listed), Sea Hunter (5f-7f), Blue Tomato (5f/6f) and Silaah (5f-7f).
runners.comment: Half-brother to 5f/6f winner Dc Flyer; dam unraced half-sister to several multiple winners

runners.name: Half Cut
runners.spotlight: 11,000euros foal, (10,000euros vendor yearling); sixth foal; half-brother to US winners Moonhall Milly (5.5f/1m) and Annaghlasa (1m); dam unraced half-sister to Italian 1m 2yo Group 3 winner Bugie D'Amore and Italian 7.5f Listed winner Sea Sex Sun; best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to two US winners; best watched on debut

runners.name: Pettus Bridge
runners.spotlight: Second foal; half-brother to 7f/1m AW winner McLean House (RPR 94); dam placed 7f (73), out of 1m2f winner, is granddaughter of Group-placed 1m6f winning sister to Derby winner Commander In Chief; others more likely.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 7f/1m AW winner McLean House (RPR 94); others more likely

runners.name: Coexist
runners.spotlight: Purchased for 320,000euros as a yearling; brother to some decent types but made no impression in a 6.5f barrier trial in August; stopped quickly and pulled up on rules debut at Dundalk in November (1m); tongue tie goes on but best watched for the moment.
runners.comment: Expensive yearling was PU on debut at Dundalk in Nov'; tongue tie on but best watched

runners.name: Four Ball
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros yearling; second foal; half-brother to 1m2f winner Heiselectric (RPR 89); dam unraced half-sister to winners Zoffman (1m2f/1m4f Flat and 2m hurdle) and Sheishybrid (12.6f/2m2f Flat and 2m1f/2m2f hurdle), out of half-sister to Gold Cup winner Big Orange; check market.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 1m2f winner Heiselectric (RPR 89); check market

runners.name: Spa Rose
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on debut at Cork last month (10.5f, soft); hard to fancy on that evidence.
runners.comment: Tailed off on debut at Cork last month (10.5f, soft); hard to fancy on that evidence

runners.name: Yangtze River
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on AW debut last month (10.5f, 125-1); unlikely.
runners.comment: Well beaten on AW debut last month (10.5f, 125-1); unlikely

runners.name: Youse Raven Mad
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on debut at Down Royal over 7f; can only be watched after that; dual headgear applied.
runners.comment: Tailed off on debut at Down Royal over 7f; can only be watched after that

runners.name: Bell Ex Too
runners.spotlight: Nice pedigree and fared okay in a strong Curragh maiden on debut on soft/heavy at the Curragh, beaten 17.5l into sixth; never better than midfield over 1m4f at Dundalk last time; more needed.
runners.comment: Nice pedigree and fared okay on turf debut; never better than midfield on AW latest

runners.name: Ravens Can Fly
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous for his Gowran debut when beaten 23l (1m, good to yielding); Mr Ballynonty looks the most appealing of the trainer's pair.
runners.comment: Never dangerous on debut; trainer runs a more appealing candidate

runners.name: Stabannon
runners.spotlight: Huge odds for Curragh debut (1m2f, good, 300-1) and finished 22l behind; unlikely.
runners.comment: Huge odds for Curragh debut (1m2f, good, 300-1) and finished 22l behind; unlikely

runners.name: Good Man Jack
runners.spotlight: Beat just one rival at Navan when 80-1 (1m2f, good to yielding); others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Beat just one rival at Navan when 80-1 (1m2f, good to yielding); others make more appeal

races.race_name: Kate O'Sullivan Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Alto Sax
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner with two of those coming here, including last week (1m2f, good) when keeping on well to win off 6lb lower under today's rider; tries this trip for the first time and should be competitive.
runners.comment: Kept on well to win off 6lb lower over 1m2f here last week; should remain competitive

runners.name: Guildhall Graduate
runners.spotlight: Slight glimmers of promise in maidens but two handicap efforts at Dundalk and Ballinrobe have been moderate; others preferred.
runners.comment: Slight promise in m'dens but two h'cap runs have been moderate; others preferred

runners.name: Tassarolo
runners.spotlight: Three wins last year, two over hurdles and 1m4f Killarney Flat handicap off 59 (soft); opening Flat runs at the Curragh (1m4f, soft to heavy) and Leopardstown (1m5f, good) were encouraging; decent fifth over 2m at Down Royal last time too.
runners.comment: Flat/hurdles winner last year has run consistent races on the level this term

runners.name: Highway Sixty One
runners.spotlight: Winless from 15 starts but there was promise in his stable debut/seasonal return at Dundalk over 1m4f last month when a 3l fourth; must build on that.
runners.comment: 0-15 but promise in stable debut/seasonal return when fourth; must build on it

runners.name: Chopsticks
runners.spotlight: 1-23 on Flat with the win coming at Sligo over an extended 1m5f in August off 51 (good to yielding); hasn't taken to hurdles since but encouragement in his close third at Dundalk over this trip last time; poor strike rate off-putting.
runners.comment: 1-23 on Flat with win coming off 51 last summer; poor strike rate off-putting

runners.name: Pebble Island
runners.spotlight: Modest form for the Crisfords and nothing so far for current connections under both codes; down 15lb already this term.
runners.comment: Modest form in Britain and nothing so far for current connections; down 15lb this term

runners.name: Wipeawayyourtears
runners.spotlight: Good start to handicap career when keeping on into second on her return at Sligo (10.5f, yielding to soft); another creditable effort over this trip at Roscommon last month when third (yielding); drops in grade now; player.
runners.comment: Good placed efforts in opening two h'caps; drops in grade now; player

runners.name: Velvet And Vine
runners.spotlight: First reserve; 0-17 and was tailed off upon return from a 500-day absence at Down Royal last month over 2m; cheekpieces go on and she drops back in trip.
runners.comment: Reserve; 0-17 and tailed off upon return from absence last time

runners.name: Awakening
runners.spotlight: With the exception of a decent effort on debut, her maiden form was moderate; well beaten on handicap debut over 1m6f at Sligo last time; cheekpieces replace the tongue tie worn that day.
runners.comment: M'den form moderate overall and well beaten on h'cap bow

runners.name: Sir Trancealot
runners.spotlight: Finished last in opening maidens at the Curragh and Dundalk and beaten 23l at Ballinrobe; cheekpieces go on for handicap bow.
runners.comment: Poor in m'dens; cheekpieces go on for h'cap bow

runners.name: Giselles Defence
runners.spotlight: Won four times for Ian Williams on a variety of ground, all at 1m2f (three times off a mark of 71); nothing for this yard until midly encouraging fifth at Bellewstown (1m, soft) on penultimate; didn't build on that at Sligo.
runners.comment: Won four times in Britain over 1m2f off higher marks; not at same level for this yard

runners.name: Magna Gee Gee
runners.spotlight: Sole Flat win came on AW at Wolverhampton in December 2024 over an extended 1m1f for Dylan Cunha; middling runs on turf this term and involved in mass run out/carry out incident at Clonmel on hurdling bow last week.
runners.comment: Sole Flat win came on AW in 2024; middling turf runs this term

runners.name: Mapa
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run well in defeat at times, including when a 3l third of 16 on seasonal return at Roscommon (1m4f, yielding to soft); never involved upped to 1m5f at Navan next time (good); she's better than that.
runners.comment: M'den has run well at times, inc' when third on seasonal return; never involved latest

runners.name: Al What
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden ran her best race at Roscommon last month when fourth of 16 (1m4f, yielding to soft); unseated on her second hurdles start at Ballinrobe next time and well beaten at Down Royal back on the level over 2m last time; this trip suits better.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose m'den ran best race over this trip last month; below that level afterwards

runners.name: Brave Approach
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three maidens before making a winning handicap debut at Dundalk in March (1m); failed to back it up in two runs back there since; takes a marked step up in trip for return to turf; 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: AW 1m winner takes a marked step up in trip for turf return.

runners.name: Premier Princess
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; no improvement seen on handicap bow last time; up in trip but others preferred.
runners.comment: Reserve; no improvement on h'cap bow last time; up in trip but others preferred

runners.name: Sofi's Gift
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in three maidens in the spring; hard to fancy now entering handicaps 3lb wrong.
runners.comment: Beaten long way in three maidens in the spring; hard to fancy entering handicaps

runners.name: Phidias Ovation
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in four maidens at 7f/1m; upped in trip for handicap bow now; check market.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in four m'dens; upped in trip for h'cap bow now; check market

runners.name: Swinging The World
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; down the field in a nursery last September for Jack Davison and tailed off on stable debut at Gowran last time, having been gelded; 8lb wrong.
runners.comment: Reserve; hasn't shown much in two h'caps to date

runners.name: Patrick Street
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose gelding added a second Flat win at Limerick in April off 44 (1m4f, yielding); respectable efforts over hurdles and fences since then; player.
runners.comment: Added second Flat win in April and respectable runs over obstacles since; player

runners.name: God Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Added third Flat win at Limerick off 73 last summer (1m5f, good); poor form following that until an encouraging 3.5l third of 14 at Navan five days ago when dropping into this grade for the first time (1m5f, good); not ruled out.
runners.comment: Dropped into this grade for first time latest and finished an encouraging third; chance

races.race_name: Sir Eric Parker Memorial EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Rollthedicebaby
runners.spotlight: 12-1 when second of six on her debut here (5f, good) six weeks ago (winner went on to be beaten a head in a Listed race at York); no problem with the extra furlong on breeding and looks the one to beat; stable also runs the newcomer Gravastar.
runners.comment: Second of six on her debut here six weeks ago and form has been franked; the one to beat

runners.name: Gravastar
runners.spotlight: April foal by Tasleet; half-sister to nine winners including Gibeon (7f 2yo/1m2f; RPR 108), Gemina (7f 2yo; 89), Girling (11.6f; 89) and Graham (1m4f; 85); dam 1m4f and 1m6f (Group 3) winner (108), out of half-sister to Gold Cup winner Fame And Glory; worth a market check, especially in relation to stablemate Rollthedicebaby, but she is another who may need further in time.
runners.comment: Worth a market check, but she is another who may need further in time

runners.name: Polgaria
runners.spotlight: March foal by Masar; 4,000gns yearling; sister to 1m4f AW winner Van Aert (RPR 73); dam Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner (85), out of 1m2f winning half-sister to US 1m/1m1f Grade 2 winner Uncharted Haven (dam of 1m4f Group 3 winner High Heeled); bred to appreciate further and others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Bred to appreciate further and others make greater appeal

runners.name: Greek Symphony
runners.spotlight: February foal by Sergei Prokofiev; half-sister to winners Indigo Dream (7f AW; RPR 84) and Royal Elysian (6f AW 2yo; 79); dam runner-up 1m AW (65), sister to useful 7f-1m2f winner Sucellus, half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Bartzella, out of 1m2f Listed winner; stable does have winning 2yo newcomers and the market should prove informative.
runners.comment: Stable does have winning 2yo newcomers and the market should prove informative

runners.name: Amethyst Star
runners.spotlight: February foal by Without Parole; £20,000 yearling; dam 9.4f AW winner (RPR 65), half-sister to winners Selino (Australian 2m Group 1), Airway (1m2f) and Always Summer (1m6f/2m), out of unraced half-sister to 2m4f Group 1 winner Invermark; stable's newcomers always worth a second look, but her pedigree suggests she may come into her own over further.
runners.comment: Stable's newcomers always worth a second look, but pedigree suggests she may need further

runners.name: Tallahassee Lassie
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred filly who showed ability when sixth of 12 on her Ascot debut (5f, good) five weeks ago, shaping as though this extra furlong would suit; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Showed ability when sixth of 12 on her Ascot debut five weeks ago; one for the shortlist

runners.name: Bearly Blue
runners.spotlight: Only seventh of ten on last month's Kempton debut (6f, AW), but was sent off just 7-2 and she did endure a wide trip; bred to be better than that and no surprise to see an improved performance.
runners.comment: Only seventh of ten on last month's Kempton debut, but bred to be better than that
runners.owner: J Henwood, E Kelly And A Bright

runners.name: Sovereign Beach
runners.spotlight: Sent off 11-2 when a well-held fifth of seven on her Ripon debut (6f, good) 15 days ago; half-sister to four winners so could do better, but will need to.
runners.comment: Fifth of seven on her Ripon debut 15 days ago; could do better, but will need to

races.race_name: Fitzdares Telephone & Text Betting Handicap

runners.name: Lavender Gold
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in all four starts as a 2yo over 7f-8.6f on turf/AW and, after having been gelded and undergone wind surgery, he looked in need of his Haydock reappearance (1m2f, good to soft) three weeks ago; looks to have a bigger performance in him.
runners.comment: Showed ability as a 2yo and looked to need his reappearance; has a bigger effort in him
runners.owner: All For One Racing Ix & Chris Wright

runners.name: Three Socks On
runners.spotlight: Won his first two AW handicaps (1m/9.5f) earlier in the year and, despite an 8lb rise, he ran well to finish fourth of 14 on turf debut at Newbury (1m2f, good) four weeks ago (third has won since); major player.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner and ran well to finish fourth of 14 on turf debut last time; major player

runners.name: Marveling
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in two 1m handicaps last month and the form of both has been boosted; wouldn't be nailed on to appreciate the longer trip, though.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in two 1m handicaps last month, but not sure to appreciate the longer trip
runners.owner: John Troy And Mrs Rebecca Philipps

runners.name: Hove Ranger
runners.spotlight: Gradually improved on the AW during the winter culminating in a narrow success at Kempton (7f) in February (form hasn't worked out); this trip should be within range, but has bit to prove back on turf after 107 days off; stable won this two years ago.
runners.comment: Won on the AW at Kempton in February, but the form hasn't worked out; off 107 days

runners.name: Semper Femina
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when second of eight on her Kempton debut in March (1m3f, AW), but hasn't built on it in two starts on turf; needs to get back on track on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Runner-up on her AW debut, but well held in two starts on turf; needs to get back on track

runners.name: Exquisite Skye
runners.spotlight: Best effort when third of 13 in a Wolverhampton maiden (8.6f, AW) for Alice Haynes in October, but two heavy defeats since (including on stable debut) leave her with plenty to prove; cheekpieces given a go on handicap debut and they need to have an effect.
runners.comment: Twice well beaten since third at Wolverhampton; cheekpieces need to have an effect

runners.name: Houndswood Willow
runners.spotlight: Hasn't finished within 12l of the winner in four starts and ran particularly poorly on handicap debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good; first-time visor) last month; needs the switch to cheekpieces to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Not come within 12l of the winner in four starts; cheekpieces need to make a difference

runners.name: Virtue Chastity
runners.spotlight: Not shown much in three AW maidens over 1m/1m2f during the winter; makes her turf/handicap debut after four months off and worth a market check.
runners.comment: Not shown much in three starts; makes her turf/handicap debut after four months off

races.race_name: Highclere Castle Gin Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Expressionless
runners.spotlight: Three of his six wins have come over this trip elsewhere and he will appreciate going back up in distance after finishing third of eight at Newmarket (1m2f, good) four weeks ago (runner-up won next time); goes on any ground and respected off the same mark.
runners.comment: Three of his six wins have come over this trip elsewhere; goes on any ground; respected

runners.name: Cruden
runners.spotlight: Only beaten a nose on his return from 114 days off over this C&D (good) last month, but finished around 6l behind Mr Freedom at Salisbury (1m6f, good) 13 days later; the likely softer ground may help.
runners.comment: Finished around 6l behind Mr Freedom at Salisbury last time; likely softer ground may help

runners.name: Aggagio
runners.spotlight: All five domestic Flat wins have come here including this race in 2022; just 1lb above his last winning mark and respected with the ground likely to be in his favour.
runners.comment: Five domestic Flat wins have come here; respected with ground likely to be in his favour

runners.name: Maasai Mara
runners.spotlight: Only beaten a neck at Brighton (1m4f, good to firm) a fortnight ago (has won on soft); 2lb higher and has to prove his stamina over this far on the Flat.
runners.comment: Only beaten a neck last time, but has to prove his stamina over this far on the Flat
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late K W Bell

runners.name: Appier
runners.spotlight: Mainly seen on the AW these days, but he did win three times on turf as a 3yo; interesting to see how he fares in the market on debut for Tony Carroll (same owner).
runners.comment: Mainly seen on AW these days but has won three times on turf; watch market on stable debut

runners.name: Mr Freedom
runners.spotlight: 11-time winner under rules who made a fine return from 389 days off when third of nine at Salisbury (1m6f, good) last month, looking unfortunate not to go closer; good or faster ground may suit ideally, but he handles soft; respected in his bid to make it 4-5 in Class 4 handicaps on the Flat.
runners.comment: Fine return last month; respected in bid to make it 4-5 in Class 4 handicaps on the Flat

runners.name: Selenic
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner (1m/1m2f) who hasn't improved for the fitting of cheekpieces and needs to put a modest effort at Kempton (2m) 16 days ago behind her; not solid on turf debut.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner who hasn't improved for the fitting of cheekpieces; not solid on turf debut
runners.owner: Sir F Brooke & Sir R Pilkington

runners.name: Premiere Ligne
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way on his return from 20 months off at Kempton (2m, AW) 16 days ago and still 6lb above his last winning mark; needs the ground to dry out.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on return from 20 months off at Kempton 16 days ago; needs faster ground

races.race_name: Fitzdares Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Overbudget
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of her five starts, the only disappointment coming on her handicap debut; return to a handicap and stepping up in trip both pose questions.
runners.comment: Placed in four of her five starts; return to a handicap and longer trip pose questions
runners.owner: Matt Broughton & Hold My Beer Syndicate

runners.name: Double Naughty
runners.spotlight: In the frame in four of her five starts for Richard Hannon as a 2yo, but a change of stable paid immediate dividends when she narrowly made a winning reappearance at Bath (5.7f, good to firm) three weeks ago (has run well on soft); up 4lb, but still one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Made a winning reappearance/stable debut at Bath last month; up 4lb but still respected

runners.name: Tashi Delek
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but her best effort came in her only start on soft ground when second of six in a 7f Yarmouth maiden last September; interesting should conditions remain testing.
runners.comment: Best effort came on soft ground when second in a Yarmouth maiden last September

runners.name: Jowalla
runners.spotlight: Placed in her first three starts over 6f/7f on turf/AW last summer, but has beaten a total of one rival in three starts in handicaps; needs to benefit from the drop in trip with a visor given a go this time.
runners.comment: Has beaten a total of one rival in three starts in handicaps; visor on

runners.name: Q T Pie
runners.spotlight: Windsor maiden winner (6f, good) for the Kublers last July, but failed to beat a rival on nursery debut at Leicester (6f, good to soft) on her final outing; makes her stable debut (same owner) after nine months off and worth a market check; stable also runs Jowalla.
runners.comment: Makes her stable debut after nine months off and worth a market check

runners.name: Perfect Location
runners.spotlight: Patchy form since winning a Southwell maiden (6f, AW) in July last year, not appearing to appreciate the step up to 7f for her last two starts; return to this trip should suit; cheekpieces replace the tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Patchy form since winning on the AW last July; return to 6f should suit; cheekpieces on
runners.owner: Mildmay Racing Pl & Only Fillies Rc

runners.name: Imagine That
runners.spotlight: Showed some improvement for the switch to turf when fifth of 12 at Salisbury (6f, good) last month; needs to progress again on handicap debut, but that is possible.
runners.comment: Fifth at Salisbury last month; needs to progress on handicap debut but that is possible

runners.name: Sierra Sue
runners.spotlight: Best effort when beaten 2l into fifth of ten in a Leicester maiden (6f, good to firm) 18 days ago, finishing a head and a neck behind Bundok; 1lb better off and worth a second look on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Fifth in a Leicester maiden 18 days ago, finishing just behind Bundok; worth a second look

runners.name: Bundok
runners.spotlight: Improved on previous efforts when beaten just over a length into third of ten in a Leicester maiden (6f, good to firm) 18 days ago; a player if coping with the likely softer ground on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Third in a Leicester maiden 18 days ago; player if coping with the likely softer ground

races.race_name: Casamigos Handicap

runners.name: Comic Strip
runners.spotlight: Both wins came in AW nurseries late last year; has shaped better than the results suggest in two runs on turf since and this drop back to 5f should be in his favour; unraced on soft.
runners.comment: Both wins on AW; has shaped better than the results suggest in two runs on turf since.

runners.name: Saffron Dandy
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut at Bath last May and landed AW wins at Lingfield and Southwell earlier this year; has been below form on her last two runs back on turf; yet to run on soft.
runners.comment: Has been below form on her last two runs back on turf.

runners.name: Wedonttelllies
runners.spotlight: Returned to winning form over C&D (good) last month, aided by first-time cheekpieces (retained since) and the drop back to the minimum trip; respectable midfield finish in the competitive 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) last Friday and holds strong claims back down in grade.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D last month and respectable midfield finish in 3yo Dash at Epsom last week

runners.name: Carefree Dream
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Bath (5f, soft) last September off 3lb lower; hasn't been in the same form since.
runners.comment: Got off the mark at Bath last September; hasn't been in the same form since.

runners.name: Killavia
runners.spotlight: Showed promise as a juvenile when third in a Listed contest at Sandown (5f, good) last July before winning her maiden; should be better for her reappearance run when sixth of eight back at Sandown (5f, good) and would go close at her best.
runners.comment: Should be better for her reappearance run when sixth at Sandown last month.

races.race_name: Download The Track Radio App Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Talis Evolvere
runners.spotlight: Five AW wins at 1m and 7f; modest strike-rate of 1-20 on turf but he's often run well in difficult races, including in one run here (hampered); well treated now but he hasn't looked in great form back on turf of late; most of best form on good or quicker, acts on good to soft.
runners.comment: Five AW wins at 1m and 7f; tempting mark now but well held back on turf of late

runners.name: Warning Sign
runners.spotlight: Two Sandown wins in 2025 at 1m and 1m2f (good and good to firm; acts on soft) under David Dunsdon; recent AW defeats can be glossed over and he's weighted to go well; one of the stronger options.
runners.comment: Two Sandown wins at 1m and 1m2f in 2025; weighted to go well; stronger than most

runners.name: Eupator
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins (about 1m and 1m1f; made all) in January; had limitations in first three turf handicaps but he was a good second at Carlisle (1m, good to firm) 13 days ago and should have a good run in him, possibly from the front again; rarely races on slow ground but ran well on heavy as 2yo.
runners.comment: Two AW wins in January; best turf form when 2nd at Carlisle (1m, good to firm) latest
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing I and Partner

runners.name: Zoffandia
runners.spotlight: Up and down on AW since these aids were fitted at the start of 2026, winning at Kempton and back to form after a couple of lesser efforts when runner-up at Lingfield in May (both 1m); no good thing to improve on poor efforts in two previous turf handicaps last year.
runners.comment: Capable but up and down on AW; well beaten in 2 turf handicaps so risky back on grass

runners.name: Arctician
runners.spotlight: Can lead; multiple AW wins at 7f and 1m but he's 0-14 on turf and it's a long time since he's been any threat on grass; up against it in only his fifth turf start in the last two years.
runners.comment: Multiple AW wins at 7f and 1m; 0-14 on turf and sparingly raced on grass now

runners.name: Post Rider
runners.spotlight: Both turf wins at about 1m (good; unraced on slower), most recently when making all at Newmarket last October; has her share of weight off 6lb higher and, as she's won on a return from a break, her recent modest Lingfield reappearance leaves some questions unanswered.
runners.comment: Both wins at 1m (good; unraced on slower); shade high in weights now

runners.name: Desperate Dan
runners.spotlight: Had an excellent spell last autumn, winning two of his final three starts at 1m (good and heavy); needed further progress to win the Spring Mile at Doncaster in March and that didn't happen but he ran well for a long way; can be more of a factor under Brodie Hampson who won this last year.
runners.comment: Won two of last three 1m starts in 2025; creditable show in the Spring Mile; can do better

runners.name: Havana Blue
runners.spotlight: All three wins at about 7f on good and good to firm; has more to prove at 1m but previous handicap attempts at the trip were in better company than this and it's likely he will stay this far round here; can go well following a creditable show at Beverley recently; Lewis Kent 2-2 for yard.
runners.comment: All 3 wins at about 7f; worth another go at 1m and should stay the trip round here

races.race_name: Alzheimer's Society Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lady Kara
runners.spotlight: Followed up her Hereford win over C&D 16 days ago, though had to work hard to do so; shoulders a double penalty and she probably has greater potential over further, but she's still the one to beat.
runners.comment: Worked hard to follow up Hereford win over C&D; double penalty, but still the one to beat.

runners.name: Colegram
runners.spotlight: Progressed well in Irish points this year, winning twice this spring on soft ground, most recently by 13l, and subsequently bought for £21,000 in May; makes rules debut for her leading stable and looks a likely type to do well over hurdles, though 2m on better ground is a different test.
runners.comment: £21,000 buy after winning second Irish point this spring; respected on hurdle/yard debut.

runners.name: Dancing Dame
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden; beaten at short odds on recent debut for James Owen at Fakenham (2m, good), but that was still a respectable effort after a break; likely to be in the mix again and worth considering.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden; respectable Fakenham debut for James Owen; likely in the mix again.

runners.name: Princess Of Ballea
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in three bumpers but got no further than the first when 25-1 on recent hurdles debut over C&D, though no fault of her own; out of a 2m hurdle winner but likely best watched for now.
runners.comment: Promise in bumpers but no luck on hurdling debut; out of a 2m hurdle winner; best watched.

runners.name: Likewhatyousee
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive over middle distances in maidens on the Flat in Ireland last year, winning her final start at the Curragh (1m4f, heavy) in October before changing hands for 60,000gns that same month; will not be inconvenienced by any rain and is a likely type to make a hurdler; makes plenty of appeal on stable debut.
runners.comment: Curragh winner (1m4f) who fetched 60,000gns; likely type to make a hurdler; respected.

runners.name: Steal A Glance
runners.spotlight: Didn't really progress from a promising start in bumpers, but her dam was a useful 2m2f-3m hurdle winner and she may improve for this switch to hurdles, though she'll need to.
runners.comment: Hurdle debut; didn't really progress in bumpers, but her dam was useful in this sphere.

runners.name: Bontessony Doll
runners.spotlight: 200-1 and, though finishing fourth over C&D last month, she was still beaten 16.5l by Lady Kara; others have more obvious claims.
runners.comment: 200-1 when fourth over C&D last month, beaten 16.5l by Lady Kara; others preferred.

runners.name: Medina Blue
runners.spotlight: Twice a runner-up in Irish points and has hinted at ability in two starts over hurdles since joining this stable, but is likely one for handicaps in due course.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up in Irish points; hinted at ability over hurdles; likely one for handicaps.

runners.name: Doobeedee
runners.spotlight: Ran well for a long way but was ultimately well held on debut in a Stratford bumper (extended 2m, good to soft; 7-1) in October; off since and makes hurdling debut with a tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Well held but shaped promisingly in an October bumper; tongue-tie on for hurdling debut.

races.race_name: William Hill Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Hurst Hill
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark when winning at this track (2m5f, good) last July off 2lb lower; runner-up three times since and didn't get the clearest of passages last time; a return to this track may well spark her back to form.
runners.comment: Got off the mark when winning at this track last July off 2lb lower.

runners.name: Jack's Jury
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on his stable debut at Ffos Las (2m4f, soft) in March with the aid of first-time cheekpieces (retained since) off 4lb lower; back over hurdles after a couple of unsuccessful efforts over fences; holds claims if running to his best form.
runners.comment: Got off the mark on his stable debut at Ffos Las in March.

runners.name: Groom De Cotte
runners.spotlight: Scored over hurdles in September 2024 and over fences in March last year; has only managed three runs over hurdles since and looks a tad high in the weights.
runners.comment: Only managed three runs over hurdles in recent times and looks a tad high in the weights.

runners.name: Final Entry
runners.spotlight: Scored on his hurdles debut in July 2024; returned to the track from a lengthy absence in February and produced his best performance to date when beaten 3l stepped up in trip at Taunton (3m, good) in April; drops down in grade here and may have to offer.
runners.comment: Produced his best performance when beaten 3l stepped up to 3m at Taunton in April.

runners.name: Bits Ballou
runners.spotlight: Has shown only modest form in three runs over hurdles; steps up in trip on handicap debut and no surprise were she to show significant improvement.
runners.comment: Has shown only modest form in three runs over hurdles; steps up in trip on handicap debut.

runners.name: Winning Paddy
runners.spotlight: 1-11 in Irish points; sold to join current yard for £2,000 in March and finished last of six on British debut, back over hurdles, at Taunton (2m3f, good; 125-1) in April; an altogether different proposition when stepped up in trip over fences when skating up here (3m2f, good; 28-1) nine days ago in cheekpieces (retained); has the same mark today back over hurdles and is a fascinating contender.
runners.comment: Stepped up in trip over fences when skating up over 3m2f here nine days ago; same mark.

races.race_name: Olympus Plant & Tool Hire Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Linden Lane
runners.spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles; beaten 33l when never in contention at Hexham (2m, soft) last month; his mark continues to drop but he looks a risky proposition at present.
runners.comment: 0-12 over hurdles; beaten 33l when never in contention at Hexham last month.

runners.name: Ask Peter
runners.spotlight: Won three on the Flat in 2023 when trained by Charlie Johnston; 0-9 over hurdles and beaten around 40l at Taunton on her last two starts; this looks easier and she may show more.
runners.comment: 0-9 over hurdles; well beaten at Taunton on her last two starts but this looks easier.

runners.name: Blue Siam
runners.spotlight: 0-15 under rules but ran her best race over hurdles when third of six finishers on her handicap debut at Stratford (2m, good to firm) 13 days ago; open to more improvement and should go close.
runners.comment: Ran her best race over hurdles when third on her handicap debut at Stratford 13 days ago.

runners.name: Minnie Belle
runners.spotlight: Beaten 37l, 52l, 43l and 21l in four hurdle runs and makes limited appeal on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Beaten 37l, 52l, 43l and 21l in four hurdle runs and makes limited appeal.

runners.name: Coup De Gold
runners.spotlight: Has a poor strike rate of 2-46 over hurdles, the last of those wins in January 2024; down to a career-low mark after an eight-month break and hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Has a poor strike rate of 2-46 over hurdles, the last of those wins in January 2024.

runners.name: Crab Road
runners.spotlight: 5yo who didn't show much at big prices in five hurdle runs (2m-2m3f) in Ireland; now makes debut for new yard and he needs a transformation.
runners.comment: 5yo who didn't show much at big prices in five hurdle runs (2m-2m3f) in Ireland.

races.race_name: execontainers.com Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Morning Mayhem
runners.spotlight: Made it four wins since joining this yard with a wide-margin success in a higher grade over 2m1f here nine days ago, achieved in little more than a canter; well treated under his 7lb penalty and he's versatile regarding ground; leading contender.
runners.comment: Four wins since joining yard, wide-margin win here last time; key player under a penalty.

runners.name: Siam Park
runners.spotlight: 4-9 over fences but yet to win in 11 starts over hurdles, although he went close under Rian Corcoran when runner-up at Worcester (2m, good) two weeks ago; up 2lb for that effort but still 3lb lower than his chase mark and another bold bid can be expected.
runners.comment: Winning chaser but yet to win in this sphere, though went close latest; respected up 2lb.

runners.name: Dream Diamond
runners.spotlight: Comfortably won a Worcester maiden (2m, good) on last summer's stable debut but has been well below that level on all three completed starts since; well held when unseating last month and has something to prove, but the first-time hood could help spark a revival.
runners.comment: Won Worcester maiden on stable debut but out of form since, hood needs to spark revival.

runners.name: Almuhit
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner, latest when readily accounting for two others at Fontwell (2m1f, good) on Wednesday when ridden by Harry Cobden; faces a quick turnaround over this slightly longer trip, but merits serious respect under his penalty.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdle winner, latest when readily winning at Fontwell on Wednesday; player.

runners.name: Donnie Azoff
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner (2m-2m5f) on varying ground; made an encouraging return when staying on for second at Hereford (2m3f, good) last month; the drop back in trip isn't ideal but he should still have a part to play.
runners.comment: Five-time hurdle winner; good second on return; drop back in trip not ideal, but chance.
runners.owner: A Walk In The Park Partnership

races.race_name: St. Austell Brewery Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Rickety Bridge
runners.spotlight: Point and hurdle winner on soft ground; has improved for the switch to fences, making it 2-2 with a strong staying performance over C&D last month, both wins coming under Freddie Gingell; up another 4lb, but he remains open to further progress and may well complete the hat-trick.
runners.comment: 2-2 since switched to fences, both over C&D ridden by Freddie Gingell; key player up 4lb.

runners.name: La Pinsonniere
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in a Huntingdon 2m maiden hurdle in January last year, but she has a string of respectable placed efforts since and wasn't beaten through lack of stamina on her first try beyond 3m latest; appears to be on a tough mark but may improve for going back over fences, having run well in France in March 2024 on her sole previous chase start; handles most ground.
runners.comment: 1-13 over hurdles but she's been consistent; return to fences could unlock improvement.

runners.name: Court In A Storm
runners.spotlight: Bumper and two-time novice hurdle winner who built on a solid sequence of placed form in staying handicap hurdles when switched to fences at Worcester (2m7f, good) a fortnight ago, jumping soundly and finishing second to a Dan Skelton-trained favourite; main concern is the forecast rain, as he has been confined to good ground.
runners.comment: Promising chase debut when second latest; capable of a big run, but rain is a concern.

runners.name: Dexperts Opinion
runners.spotlight: Deep-ground 2m2f hurdle winner in France last summer but his form dipped soon after, including in one start over fences; however, he made the perfect start for his new stable when landing a Worcester handicap hurdle (2m7f, good) last month off a 5lb lower mark and looks the type to do better over fences; interesting contender.
runners.comment: French hurdle winner who added to that success on stable debut at Worcester; interesting.

races.race_name: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Beorma
runners.spotlight: Rapidly improving mare, having won three chases and two hurdles (2m4f/2m) on good ground in the space of a fortnight, latest at Worcester yesterday on the bridle and value for markedly more than the winning margin; respected assuming she again turns out quickly, although any significant easing of the ground would be a concern.
runners.comment: Rapidly improving mare with five wins in a fortnight; major threat but rain a concern.

runners.name: Cobbler's Boy
runners.spotlight: Came good over fences when winning over C&D in May and may have found his follow-up bid, when a respectable second back over hurdles just seven days later, coming too soon; revised mark has now kicked in back over fences but he still commands respect.
runners.comment: C&D chase winner in May; respectable hurdle second since, up in weights but respected.

runners.name: First Angel
runners.spotlight: Lightly campaigned in recent years but a three-time chase winner for this yard, all around 2m on ground no worse than good to soft; shaped as though he would improve for March's comeback after another long absence and has a history of doing just that; forecast rain is the main concern.
runners.comment: Three-time chase winner who should improve for latest return; rain the main concern.

races.race_name: Racing To School Reaches 25 Years Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Park Hall
runners.spotlight: Won an Irish point (3m, yielding) on debut last month and subsequently changed hands for £27,000; that form looks solid enough and she's bred to do well under rules, being the first foal of an unraced dam who is a half-sister to useful staying hurdler Jungle Prose; hood fitted for this rules debut and represents a leading stable; respected.
runners.comment: £27,000 Irish point winner; hooded rules debutant from a leading yard; respected.

runners.name: Unflappable
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to 2m-2m1f chase winner Pure Carbon; had her supporters and shaped with promise when third over C&D (good) on last month's debut; that experience should stand her in good stead and she's open to improvement; commands respect.
runners.comment: Promising C&D third on debut last month; open to improvement and respected.

runners.name: Blinded By Grace
runners.spotlight: Improved on a promising debut when winning at Uttoxeter (2m, good) last month, showing a good attitude to assert close home; key player under a penalty.
runners.comment: Uttoxeter winner last month who showed a good attitude; key player under a penalty.

runners.name: Poppycat
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to four winners, notably Group 2 scorer Ahzeemah (7f-2m), smart performer Austrian School and dual Grand National hero Tiger Roll; appeals strongly on paper given that depth of pedigree and market support would heighten interest; one to keep a close eye on making her debut.
runners.comment: Well-bred newcomer; half-sister to four winners including Tiger Roll; market best guide.

runners.name: Petite Poet
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to bumper winner Queenofthejukebox; dam an unraced sister to 3m chase scorer Glengar; appeals on pedigree and should have a future, but likely one for the longer term and may be best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Fifth foal; pedigree offers some appeal but likely one for later and best watched.

runners.name: Let's Have A Drink
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam a point winner, half-sister to winners Definitelyanoscar (bumper/2m hurdle) and Towering (2m-2m7f hurdle); yard 0-11 in bumpers in the last five seasons and he may be one for further down the road.
runners.comment: Pedigree offers hope but yard 0-11 in bumpers in the last five seasons; one for later.

races.race_name: Hayley Middleton Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tiny Riot
runners.spotlight: 60-rated on the Flat; won the first juvenile hurdle of the season at Hexham (2m, good; 3-1) last Saturday, a race which also featured Cracking Anna (fourth) and Steel Fixer (pulled up); should confirm his superiority over those rivals despite the incurred 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Won first juvenile hurdle of the season at Hexham (2m, good) last Saturday; 7lb penalty.

runners.name: Cracking Anna
runners.spotlight: Limited on the Flat (40-rated); cautious hurdles debut at Hexham (2m, good; fourth to Tiny Riot) last Saturday but she ran her race, beaten just over 6l, and she's entitled to draw benefit for that; the task isn't an impossible one.
runners.comment: Cautious hurdles debut at Hexham (fourth to Tiny Riot) last Saturday; entitled to improve.

runners.name: Vietnorm
runners.spotlight: 2-2 on the Flat (1m2f; now rated 56) since being gelded, narrowly beating the frustrating Bradbury both occasions; dam was an unraced half-sister to the useful 2m-2m3f hurdle winner Daybreak Boy among others; interesting to see the level of market confidence behind him.
runners.comment: 2-2 on the Flat (1m2f; now rated 56) since being gelded; consult the market for advice.

runners.name: Steel Fixer
runners.spotlight: 52-rated Flat maiden; headed before two out, weakened quickly and pulled up (reportedly made a respiratory noise) in the race won by Tiny Riot at Hexham (2m, good; 14-1) on Saturday; with that in mind, even with a tongue-tie added, this represents a quick turnaround.
runners.comment: Pulled up (reportedly made respiratory noise) behind Tiny Riot at Hexham (2m) on Saturday.

races.race_name: Rase Vets Summer Party Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Jessie Jump Jet
runners.spotlight: Yet to win (0-11) but she couldn't have gone much closer at Warwick (2m5f, good; 16-1) on penultimate start, and backed that up with another solid effort here (2m3f) a fortnight ago; stamina is the question mark over this trip.
runners.comment: Runner-up on last two starts, including here (2m3f) last time; stamina is the risk factor.

runners.name: Newmill Getaway
runners.spotlight: Still seeking her first hurdles win (0-13) but she has been placed in over half of those attempts, including when returning from a two-month break at Bangor (2m7f, good; third, beaten over 4l) latest; trainer/jockey combination won this last year with Casting Aspersions.
runners.comment: Third on return from a two-month break at Bangor (2m7f, good; beaten over 4l) latest.

runners.name: Brionywells
runners.spotlight: Arrives seeking a hat-trick following wins at Worcester (2m7f; 20-1) and Uttoxeter (2m4f; 11-4); carries a 7lb penalty for that latest win - 14lb higher than when the winning spree commenced - but every chance of going in again in a race of this nature.
runners.comment: Arrives seeking a hat-trick following wins at Worcester (2m7f) & Uttoxeter (2m4f); player.

runners.name: Slack Alice
runners.spotlight: Gradual improvement over hurdles, twice hitting the frame at Ludlow (2m5f-3m), getting to within 3l of Son Of Tyran (beaten by Brionywells since) last month; slightly more needed to make the breakthrough from this 1lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Hit the frame at Ludlow (2m5f-3m) on last two runs, getting to within 3l of winner latest.

runners.name: Popa Rich
runners.spotlight: Twice placed at Cartmel (2m6f) last summer; this year's form figures PF aren't overly inspiring though, so it's difficult to argue a reasoned case, unless the newly applied cheekpieces have a majorly uplifting impact.
runners.comment: This year's form figures PF aren't overly inspiring; difficult to argue a reasoned case.

runners.name: Ramaah
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on AW at Chelmsford (13.5f) back in August 2024; beaten 16l, 36l, and 38l on his last three completed hurdle starts; makes no appeal whatsoever over this new trip from 2lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Beaten 16l, 36l, & 38l on last three completed hurdle starts; makes no appeal whatsoever.

runners.name: Swingingonthesteel
runners.spotlight: Dual bumper winner (peak RPR 102); turned over at short odds on hurdles debut at Sedgefield (2m1f, maiden) last November and things haven't got any better since; tongue-tie is added for handicap debut (appropriate level) but with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Dual bumper winner; struggled over hurdles; handicap debut, arriving with plenty to prove.

runners.name: Mc'flo
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who has yet to show any worthwhile hurdles form; finished 28l behind today's rival Brionywells at Worcester (2m7f; 33-1) a fortnight ago; tongue-tie now discarded; better to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who has yet to show any worthwhile hurdles form; look elsewhere.

races.race_name: Rebecca Brooks Handicap Chase

runners.name: Get A Superstar
runners.spotlight: Knuckled down to get the better of the favourite in a tight finish here (2m6f, good; 7-2) a fortnight ago; that was his first win since his bumper days, so a nominal 3lb rise seems fair; dropping back in trip is unlikely to pose too much of a problem.
runners.comment: Knuckled down to score in tight finish here (2m6f, good; 7-2) a fortnight ago; 3lb higher.

runners.name: Prospect House
runners.spotlight: Market Rasen regular, with three wins, two of those in a visor over fences (2m1f); just did enough to prevail at Catterick (2m; from 2lb lower) in December and looked in need of the comeback run at Cartmel (weakened quickly) 16 days ago; don't discount.
runners.comment: Market Rasen regular - two chase wins here (2m1f); looked in need of Cartmel comeback run.
runners.owner: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing

runners.name: Ask A Sainte
runners.spotlight: Ex David Brace; travelled strongly, winning readily on second start for this trainer over hurdles at Uttoxeter (2m, good; 5-6 favourite) eight days ago; is due a 9lb rise, but switches to fences for the first time (half-brother to the 2m chase winner Thatza Dazzler) from an unchanged mark; obvious chance.
runners.comment: Ready winner over hurdles at Uttoxeter (2m; 5-6 fav) eight days ago; switches to fences.

runners.name: Tsavo Park
runners.spotlight: Yet to win (0-12 rules) and his form figures over fences (FP8) aren't convincing; that said; he would have at least made the frame but for a final-fence fall at Thurles (2m2f; soft) in February; badly out of sorts on last two starts, so will need the cheekpieces to revitalise.
runners.comment: 0-12; badly out of sorts on last two starts; will need the cheekpieces to revitalise.

runners.name: Blue Bear
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 19 came at Sedgefield (2m1f, soft; from 15lb higher) in February 2024; returns to fences for the first time in a while, after checking out swiftly over hurdles at Hexham (2m; fifth, beaten over 16l) last month; others more obvious.
runners.comment: Checked out swiftly over hurdles at Hexham (2m; fifth, beaten 16l+) last month; unlikely.

runners.name: Fast Deal
runners.spotlight: 1-66, that a 150-1 shock at Stratford (3m4f) in April 2024; nearly as many letters as numbers in his form figures since January 2025, tailed off (suffered from heat stress) on return to action at Cartmel three weeks ago; competes from 9lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: 1-66; tailed off (heat stress) on return at Cartmel three weeks ago; 9lb wrong here.

races.race_name: Bob And Babs Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Maximum Offers
runners.spotlight: Has won two small-field chases over 3m1f on good ground at Hereford since returning from a winter break and being tried in cheekpieces; up 7lb; down in trip but a contender.
runners.comment: Two small-field chase wins over 3m1f on last two starts; up 7lb; should go well again
runners.owner: L H & A Snowden & J E Snowden

runners.name: King Roly
runners.spotlight: Three wins at around 2m4f on good ground in the first half of last season; hasn't recaptured his form after a winter break, last time pulling up at Stratford after jumping right; this track should suit him better.
runners.comment: Three wins last season but not at his best this term; this right-handed track will suit

runners.name: Wudya
runners.spotlight: Winner over hurdles off a 3lb higher mark at Bangor last July (2m3f, good); not disgraced when 20l fourth on same track after a break on chase debut last month (2m3f, good); should improve for that run and one to consider.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles last July; fair chase debut after a break last time; one to consider

runners.name: Fringill Dike
runners.spotlight: All eight jump wins have been at around 2m; lost his chance by jumping right when stepped up to 2m4f at Aintree last time (finished a well-beaten sixth); will be better going right-handed; 21lb lower than for his last win (August 2024) and stable in form, but a bit to prove.
runners.comment: All wins at around 2m (yet to prove as effective over further); well treated; bit to prove

runners.name: Okavango Delta
runners.spotlight: Won twice in 2025, at Musselburgh in January (2m4f, soft) and at Worcester in May (2m4f, good); hasn't done as well since and was pulled up at Stratford last time; could bounce back
runners.comment: Two wins over 2m4f last year (soft/good); hasn't done as well recently; could bounce back

runners.name: Hurricane Bay
runners.spotlight: Not particularly consistent but won at Huntingdon early last month (2m4f, good) and would have a chance on that form; however, pulled up when favourite at Fakenham last time having reportedly made a respiratory noise.
runners.comment: Has a chance on last month's Huntingdon win; pulled up with respiratory problem last time

runners.name: Doyouknowwhatimean
runners.spotlight: Creditable 14l fourth at Bangor in March (2m4f, good); pulled up on next two starts and had been pulled up on his two starts before that run, so is hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Creditable fourth at Bangor in March but that was his only completion in last five starts

races.race_name: Rase Veterinary Summer Party Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Trust House
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (evens favourite) in March and followed up at Ffos Las (2m, good) in April; played second fiddle to stablemate Castle Ivers at Perth (2m; 7-4 favourite; from 3lb lower) latest, but there was still plenty of merit to that; is now rated 19lb higher than when the winning spell commenced.
runners.comment: Played second fiddle to stablemate Castle Ivers at Perth (2m; 7-4 fav; 3lb lower) latest.

runners.name: Ip Up
runners.spotlight: No win since January 2024; consistent (43322) last autumn/winter; tried to make all at Kelso (2m, good) 19 days ago but faded from the second-last, finishing over 15l fourth to the subsequent Perth winner Scots Poet; 3lb lower, with headgear added.
runners.comment: Tried to make all at Kelso (2m) latest but faded from the second-last; headgear is added.

runners.name: Kingston Sunflower
runners.spotlight: 5-13 over hurdles, including back-to-back wins for Fern O'Brien at Ludlow and Huntingdon (both 2m) last October/November; fresh and keen on recent comeback run at Warwick (2m, good; sixth of seven, beaten nearly 15l); this Class 4 looks a better fit, assuming the rain stays away.
runners.comment: Fresh and keen on recent comeback run at Warwick (2m, good; sixth); this is a better fit.

runners.name: Pigeon House
runners.spotlight: Made all, winning unchallenged in a Catterick claimer (2m, good) in March, changing hands for £12,000; failed to beat a rival on the Flat at Southwell (1m3f) on first start for this trainer the following month, and pulled up on chase debut next time; returning to hurdles is a sensible move.
runners.comment: Pulled up on Bangor chase debut (made mistakes); returning to hurdles is a sensible move.

races.race_name: Rase Equine Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Little Ledgend
runners.spotlight: 0-9 under rules; knocking firmly at the door through the winter, collared close home over 2m7f here (soft; third, 15-8 favourite) in February; reappears from a 115-day break from a 2lb higher mark, but with Lewis Saunders claiming a handy 3lb; tongue-tie added and drop back in trip is helpful.
runners.comment: 0-9; collared close home over 2m7f here (soft; third, 15-8 fav) in February; 2lb higher.

runners.name: Born Like Me
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; returning from a near five-month absence when finishing a weakening fifth of ten to Banderas at Hexham (2m4f, good; 22-1) 24 days ago; now 3lb lower but in need of sharp improvement.
runners.comment: Weakening fifth of ten to Banderas at Hexham (2m4f, good; 22-1) 24 days ago; 3lb lower.

runners.name: Bunker Bay
runners.spotlight: Usually saves his best for this track, third to Trust House (runs in the 8.01pm) in March and runner-up to a subsequent winner in April, prior to gaining his first win over hurdles here (2m; good, 4-1) a fortnight ago; a 6lb higher mark (close to a career-high) will make life tougher.
runners.comment: Gained his first win over hurdles here (2m; good, 4-1) a fortnight ago; now 6lb higher.

runners.name: Knacker Trapper
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner (1-23) but has finished in the frame in three of his last four starts, going down by a short-head to the determined Heart Above at Kelso (2m5f, good) 19 days ago; bit more needed from this 2lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Went down by a short-head to Heart Above at Kelso (2m5f, good) 19 days ago; 2lb higher.

runners.name: Zuul
runners.spotlight: 20-race maiden; bled from the nose and soundly beaten over fences at Sedgefield (2m5f) last October; returned with a creditable third to Evenwood Sonofagun over hurdles at Cartmel (2m6f; 20-1) 18 days ago; competes from 2lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: 0-20; back with creditable third at Cartmel (2m6f; 20-1) 18 days ago; 2lb out of handicap.

runners.name: Estacas
runners.spotlight: No win for three years but, in all fairness, he hasn't seen a massive amount of action, and he's handily weighted from this 6lb lower mark; bumped into the thriving Ice Jet at Worcester (2m4f; runner-up, beaten over 1l) latest, and a repeat of that should see him on the premises.
runners.comment: Bumped into the thriving Ice Jet at Worcester (2m4f; runner-up, btn 1l+) latest; player.

runners.name: Roxboro River
runners.spotlight: Surprise Stratford winner (2m6f, good to firm; from this mark) last July; failed to build on that thereafter; more encouraging signs when fourth (beaten over 5l) back at that track 13 days ago; isn't guaranteed to replicate that here though, and the prospect of showers concerns.
runners.comment: Encouraging signs when fourth (beaten over 5l) at Stratford 13 days ago; can he replicate?
